review.text: Small, but big flavors. Menu del dia here is a very good bargain.

Probably the best airport hotel in the world. Literally you just walk over from the hotel into terminal 3 (or vice versa). No sketchy connections at 2am, just a bridge, then sleep.

Breakfast is Halal, and absolutely wonderful (even if you’re a heathen bacon connoisseur like myself). The bar will serve you 1l beers as long as you can stand. Rooms are massive (especially the suites). Anyway, finally a hotel that does the Marriott/Bonvoy marque some positives.
Garbage airport where security takes 90 minutes. Worst in the EU I’ve ever been thru.

Everyone knocking this place is whack. It’s a tourist oriented restaurant in the tourist area. They didn’t charge any cover, drinks were a bit slow, but not the end of the world. It’s also significantly cheaper than other bars around the corner
Gotta check the ✅. Take the terassa tour and take the stairs. You’ll feel good about yourself the next day for getting a bit of exercise.
The cats here are soft and sweet. They nap a lot, but they’re still active.

Typical airport tourist trap. “I want a burger, no fries” proceeds to bring me a burger with fries. “The burger doesn’t come with fries.” “Yes I asked for just the burger no fries”.

$19 multiplies, that’s how it be in Jamaica.
Always has stuff on sale.
Beware it’s always a zoo and very crowded. If I were a local I’d avoid it like the plague. As a tourist, you’ll love it.
One of the coolest adaptive reuse projects in the world!
What a cool conference venue. Spacious & intimate.

Sullen staff, lousy food, no possibility to get a res at any of the restaurants all week because the app is glitchy, 2+ hrs waiting for room service (sandwiches) that never came (sandwiches and salad are the only offer), lousy internet, and paper thin walls.

I’m sure this place is magical to many. It simply isn’t my speed. The issues with the food service is just the icing on the cake.
Food is decent, only problem is it takes 30-40 min for anything unless you show up at opening. Want to eat dinner? Stand in line 30 min for the “normal buffet”.

Very "meh" property in my experience. On a 4 night stay with 4 "restaurant credits", only 1 night is available for a "fancy restaurant" reservation when I checked in (at 6pm... yay, Italian... the lowest level of all "nice food at a resort"). Kinda sucks at what they charge but... you’ll still wait 20-30 min in line to get seated for dinner? Want an alternate! Guess again because there’s no room service.

That's cool, it's a "24 hour" property with "round the clock room service" so I can just order whatever whenever for my jet-lagged ass; The only caveat is they don't answer the phones before ~7/8am... so if you're an early riser (or jetlag challenged like me at 5am) you simply have to wait until the breakfast is open anyway. I know it's island time, but again at $300/ night for 1 person... the experience is simply lackluster even with Globalist status (as it's a Hyatt associated property).

Also, the hostess working at the lunch seriously sucks if you're a single patron. I've asked a couple times to have a seat on the water - "no... tables dirty" not going to get into the politics, but yea... thumbs down from this dude who is at Hyatt properties for over 100 nights a year.

Kinda bummed to dock the stars so low because the food is decent (not amazing), but the service is terrible. I came at a low traffic hour for lunch; normal dress and all. Hostess seats me in the crumbiest high traffic corner even though there's 20% occupancy. I ask if I can have one of many other tables but "no, they're dirty.." The server assigned to me even looked on kinda confused and offered to move me. Just another letdown with this property.
If you can't reserve it on a 4 day stay... it's a 1 star.

Great hotel with 1 small caveat - the lobby is absolutely filthy. Normally this isn’t a problem as I don’t chill in the lobby, but I had an early flight into london and my room wasn’t ready yet. After sitting in the chairs downstairs, I burned my pants because the chairs were so gross.

My main complaints are nit picky because this place is dirt cheap and has a lot going for it all things considered. The main complaint is that it’s a bit dated seeming.

But, they have a nice car share deal for 10£. The food in the pub is pretty good (albeit at hotel prices), and the room they gave me was enormous.

I’d definitely stay here again.
Def a decent spot. Great happy hr prices, nice bar outside and tables as well.

It’s a solid 4. Not amazing, not shit. The eggs were crumbly, but the sausage was all right. Rooms are large, but a bit dated. It’s a place for a quick airport layover, but if you have to take an uber there, that’ll be $30 for a 10 min ride and all. You get it.
Great location, nice sized apartment as listed. I would definitely stay here again. I left my airpods when I checked out and they held them for me over the weekend until I was passing back through.
The people working here are always really nice even with my broken Spanish. They also have the best boquerones and very cold beer glasses
Always a nice spot to have lunch. They usually have really good delivery specials as well.

pin.name: Four Points Flex by Sheraton London Shoreditch East

review.text: Place is dirt cheap so really hard to beat if you have a meeting or something in the area. Don’t stay here for your honeymoon, the views are terrible. But if you just need a bed to crash for 8 hrs, this is the right spot.

Probably my new favorite hotel in central London for business trips. Super convenient location, staff are awesome, and it’s a CitizenM so the room is thoughtfully arranged.

Probably not a great spot for more than 1 person though, it’s tight and the bathroom has (frosted) glass all over which is a big no for me travelling with someone else.
The food is always great at Sticks, but the staff working here make this location one of my favorite.

aspectRatings.noise_level: Loud, but you can still talk

review.text:
Visited multiple times. I guess I looked “off” today because I couldn’t get a table even though it was incredibly early and others before/after me were able 🤷‍♂️.

Food is good (I’ve eaten here many times), meh on the staff this month. If service is terrible the juice isn’t worth the squeeze.

Not only is the coffee brilliant, but the staff working here are exceedingly kind. Being my typical clumsy self I spilled coffee everywhere. They were nice enough to make me a new coffee and wouldn’t let me pay for it. Definitely a great spot!
Always the best meal in Cordoba!
Nice spot with an interior terrace. Great place to hang out with some friends!
Pies come out quick, and prices are very reasonable. The dough has a nice chew, but crunches. I’d def stop by here again.
The Saturday happy hour is awesome! Patio is a little drab, but the food & prices are great!

aspectRatings.noise_level: Loud, but you can still talk

review.text: Wait staff were rude and didn’t want to seat us because we rode in on bicycles 🙄. Couldn’t tell you how the food is, it’d been 5+ years since I’ve visited so can’t really vouch for the quantity when we weren’t even allowed to sit.
One of my favorite spots to chill in Houston. Tons of games, taco trucks are lit, and the beer is cold!
It’s really hard to beat the prices for happy hour. Super chill spot to have a beer with your friends!
Typical dealership experience with lots of games and added fees

It’s a very meh lounge. The internet is garbage. If you have free access there’s nothing to complain about. If you’re paying the day rate, it’s not great.

Drinks are always warm, and almost no ice available. Sometimes things run out as well. It’s not a bad place, just not good for a flagship airport lounge.

Edit, I had to cut another star. The internet will drop you every ~5 minutes which is unfortunate if you have emails and such to send. Don’t count on taking a call during a layover on a business trip.

“You want fries or onion rings?”
“No thanks”
Food shows up with fries + $5.50 extra charge. They aren’t even good fries.

I love being disappointed. If you do too, you’ll love this place. Unfortunately for everyone else, it’s the only option here. Yay monopolies!!!

pin.name: Residence Inn by Marriott Lincoln Downtown

review.text: Rooms are incredibly spacious, this might be my new fav spot in downtown Lincoln.

pin.name: The Scarlet, Lincoln, a Tribute Portfolio Hotel

review.text: Had a super late night arrival due to a delayed flight. Front desk agent was incredibly hostile seeming (especially for Lincoln) when I checked in. My night of sleep was decent enough and the room was great.

pin.name: Fairfield by Marriott Inn & Suites Lincoln Airport

review.text: Super nice for a Fairfield. I’d stay here again the night before a flight. Breakfast is meh, but that’s typical for Fairfield.
The Hard Product is definitely showing its age, but it’s not a terrible hotel. The bathrooms are on the tiny side, and 8 foot ceilings are a bit depressing today but here we are.

pin.name: Four Points Flex by Sheraton Madrid Atocha

review.text: Tight quarters, but very cheap. Bathrooms have one of those weird doors that is half shower door half restroom door and doesn’t close all the way.
Excellent burgers, fast turnaround!
Definitely one of the best Courtyards in the ecosystem. The staff are really on point and the hotel exceeds the hard product expectation of the flag.

This property definitely needs some tlc and has seen better days. Rooms are basic, but functional. The biggest problem at this hotel is the WiFi. Not a big deal normally but when your stay is 80% rainy weather, Netflix buffers every 5 minutes or you’ll drop phone calls talking to your mom.
If you go up the mountain, you might as well go in as it’s included in your ticket. There are a bunch of older guns, photos, and other relics from a battle no one alive remembers
Great views, if you’re not skiing, bring the right clothes, it’s cold!
Crazy to find Georgian food up in the mountains. The guy running the place was super nice. Definitely a great spot on a cold day.
Food is good, but very greasy
What a fun place to see all sorts of nature things! I went because I had an extra museumkaart visit to spend. Worth the trip on the train over for sure
This place may be my new favorite restaurant near all the tourist traps in Venice. It’s a half block around the corner from the main drag and a bit hidden, but it’s worth the patience of finding it.
Lovely place to soak and relax in the pools. Even if it’s snowing, you’ll be warm.

pin.name: Lindner Hotel Vienna Am Belvedere - JDV by Hyatt

review.text: Hotel is in a great location for seeing Vienna. The downside is there was no bathroom door.
Great spot to go with someone into microscopes. They have a lot and you can look at all the tiny things thru them.
Don’t skip Murano when visiting Venice. It’s only a short vaporetto ride away and a lot more calm and relaxed than other parts of the lagoon.
Place is always a great spot to snag lunch. Dining area is a bit small and shared with the kitchen so some smells may cling to your clothes. Food is fast, fresh, and delicious!
Amazing pizza, great prices. Be aware there’s usually a line.

It’s no longer a book store you can browse (the price of overtures), but they still have cats in the trees. Be aware entering is like jumping on the on ramp of a crazy one way walkway with books rather than browsing a book store. You have to go one way and it’s more set for insta photos than selling books now which is sad.
Food and location are great. Be aware it’s a typical tourist place where everything you say yes to is an upcharge (bread,water, etc). If you go in knowing how things work here you’ll love it.
Great spot, no cover, no catty smell, and the cats are all friendly and well groomed. Coffee was pretty good as well! Def one of the better cat cafes out there!
Really helpful staff, super knowledgeable about watches as well.

Overall, I felt that this property is a 3 star hotel cosplaying as a 5 star hotel. The good: it's an easy location right off the train station, the rooms are relatively large, and the water pressure is high, and the views are nice.

Now for the downsides:

For the hard product: This is a hotel without any dividing spaces for the bathroom - ie the toilet has a glass door and it opens into the main room (why do hotels do this?). My room did not have soap in it either. Not what you want when you get in at 1am then have to go ask for the basics from reception.

As for the soft product, it was about the same. Expect to wait 30+ minutes to get breakfast. When you're seated, you'll wait another 30 minutes to receive your order, then 40+ minutes just to get your coffee. Lines to check out are 20+ minutes long in the morning, and service for the room came after it was dark.
It smells so heavenly in here!
Chicken was ✨✨✨. I would come eat here again.
Meal was pretty decent, it’s part of the hotel so if you have IHG food vouchers you can use them which is pretty cool.

aspectRatings.noise_level: Loud, but you can still talk

review.text: Pinned this spot years ago. Turns out I was a €15 taxi away this morning so I figured “what else am I doing today” and dropped in. What a cool spot!
Blue skies!!!
Amazing blue skies in December!
Definitely reserve a time in advance online, half of the line didn’t and was not able to visit.

pin.name: Holiday Inn - the niu, Dairy Haarlem by IHG

review.text:
Decent enough spot. It’s about 8/9 min from the Haarlem station so fairly easy to get to.

In terms of status/loyalty, they did not offer the complementary breakfast, but I did get a free beer as an IHG diamond member.
The orange cats 🧡 so special 😍
The prices are a bit steep for Larnaca but the food was decent enough. The cats in front are very friendly as well. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ rating for the cat portion.
Nice spot on the corner with outdoor seating. Food was on the salty side, but so is most of life. I’d come back and eat here again.

pin.name: Labtwentytwo Barcelona, a Tribute Portfolio Hotel

review.text: Very “meh” hotel for me. The bathroom door was broken and they couldn’t seem to get it fixed the entire stay. Also there’s no tortilla at breakfast in Spain? Rooftop pool looks nice, but you can’t even sit around it in the offseason even on nice days.

Firmly in the meh category for me. Room wasn’t ready at check in, staff had us sit around and wait then had a shift change and forgot about us. They don’t sweep for a “heavy” cleaning so after 5 days the tiny rooms are filthy.
Definitely a relaxing spot. Best if you have a car, but doable for a foot passenger if you plan on mostly relaxing at the resort.
It’s really hard to go out for under 50€ and have a better meal imo. My only gripe is the knives in particular really could use a sharpening.

pin.name: Hyatt Centric City Centre Kuala Lumpur

review.text:
Great spot right in the center of things but not jam packed with pedestrian traffic. Rooftop bar prices were high and the bar isn’t open till 4pm which is a bummer, but not the end of the world.

Re rooms, not a fan of disconnected toilet/ bathroom but not a huge deal either. I’d stay again.
Cool spot, a bit pricey for the entrance, but there are lots of cats around.

The food is decent enough, but I wouldn’t go out of my way to return. Be prepared to wait an incredibly long time and pay Western European capital tourist prices. Food took the better part of an hour and there were only 2 other tables filled when we arrived. The elevator also smelled of sewage.
Apparently I visited on the best of days. The view is great, prices are in line with a European tourist spot, and the food I ordered was delivered quickly and was very good. Ymmv

Amazing doner!!!

Edit: A year later and very much meh. Prices are high, doner is dry.
One of my favorite spots in the city to get out of the hustle and bustle
They’ve got the chillest cat. They also sell beers and such cheaper than the resort next door.
Meal was great, service was prompt, prices were reasonable. I’d eat here again.

pin.name: La Juiceria Superfoods Signature • Nadi Bangsar

review.text: Legit good stuff. Prices are great, food is cooked really quickly, what more can you want?
Really nice setup for events
Anything you wanna eat, right here.
This is a good place to find cats.

pin.name: 伊勢丹 Isetan The Japan Store

review.text: Prob my fav place to grab some takeaway in KUL. All of it’s great and the prices are super reasonable.
Fit the bill for an airport stay. Note it’s not super close (walking) to the terminals.

pin.name: Andaz Munich Schwabinger Tor, by Hyatt

review.text: It’s sooo orange. I don’t know if good or bad. Pool and breakfast  are legit. Rooms can be a bit of a hike due to the property layout.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Muscat al Seeb by IHG

review.text: This is a very pleasant hotel. I stayed here on an 18 hour layover. Location was perfect to get 8 hours (not in an airplane seat) then have a quick city tour in the morning before heading east. Pool is nice and internal to hotel, rooms were large with blackout curtains, and ac was super refreshing.
A bit off the beaten track but pleasant enough.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express Munich Airport - Erding by IHG

review.text:
Best idea ever - stay here after your international flight to Germany, it’s reasonably priced, checks all the boxes, and you’re gunna feel like shit anyway so why bother going somewhere far or fancy…

Here are more reasons why: it’s close to the airport, bed is nice, you can hit the shower, then you’re optimally positioned to have a legit spa day (5 min taxi), followed by a walk to a delicious German meal (with an Erdinger to wash it down of course) in a small town away from tourists. Then go to Munich the next day.
One of my fav places to spend a Sunday.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Istanbul - Old City by IHG

review.text: Hotel is a couple streets off the hustle and bustle.

I’m a huge fan of this hotel. It’s smaller and feels boutique, all rooms are floor 35+, the pool is more indoor solarium than outdoor Infiniti style and never has kids, right on the train, and lots of stuff within a 10 minute drive to eat.
One of those can’t miss places in Istanbul. It’s free to walk around so why wouldn’t you stop by and spend an hour or so wandering? And there’s a lot of cats!!!
It’s a shopping mall, the ac is great.
Way better than any bw3 in the west. The food was pretty good and didn’t have the “obviously reheated frozen wing” taste you get in the states.
This place is like a small oasis from the heat. Super quick, reasonable prices, and clean.

pin.name: Gasthaus & Hotel zum ERDINGER Weißbräu Erding

review.text: The food is great. The service not so much. Trying to pay I get no acknowledgement from my server as I try to flag him down 3/4 times (after ignoring me but taking orders from others at my table) I get barked at. Meh…

This place is pretty dope.
Madori was awesome! She took care of all our sushi needs!
Nice garage, signs are very clear
Always a convenient place to stay if you need easy access to the airport. Rooms are funky and the bathroom is open concept, but the skygarden is sweet.

pin.name: Hyatt Place Las Vegas at Silverton Village

review.text: Beautiful views, but the hotel is pretty nice overall. Typical Hyatt place experience and they have free parking!
Kinda a goofy crew working here
Bridge that’s painted like Legos, don’t buy tickets for this, it’s just a bridge
Really nice lowkey spot for a drink. Views are epic.

aspectRatings.noise_level: Loud, but you can still talk

review.text: Fast, greasy, delicious. What more could you want?
Fast, many languages spoken, reasonable prices. I’d eat here again.

pin.name: Hotel Indigo Antwerp - City Centre, an IHG Hotel

review.text: Great location, but this property is definitely showing its age. The “couch” ie window sill seat padding was literally falling apart, and the faux fur blankets used to cover were tired and made the shedding worse. Bathroom was also a bit musty and in need of recaulking.

pin.name: Holiday Inn - the niu, Tab Dusseldorf Main Station, an IHG Hotel

review.text:
This is a hotel I’d really like to like. The guys working at the bar are especially nice and the downstairs area with games is 🔥.

“Dedicated workspace” means the tv stand doubles as the desk. The side table is a stool from a kindergarten so if you’re using the desk you have to take all your stuff off the “side table”. This alone knocks a property off my list. Particularly when the images of the room I booked on the app show a real chair.

Air Conditioning does not work. If you plan to stay in the summer you’ll sweat. If you open the windows you get to listen to the kids outside and their shenanigans.

Positive point, this place is like 50-60€/ night. For what I spent, I can’t really complain all that much.
It’s a train, it takes you places you may or may not need to go. This one runs over the streets but under the tracks. Imagine that.
Competent hotel, breakfast is pretty basic and premium rooms aren’t that big but no real overall complaints.

pin.name: Me and All Hotel Ulm, by Hyatt

review.text:
Location is key when selecting a hotel. This one is directly across the train station. If Ulm is on a "Rail Trip Across Europe" trip, this is an amazing spot. Judging from appearance of the others eating breakfast "quick business trip to Ulm" is more likely. Still an amazing location if that's the case.
If you're here on business you're probably flying solo, in which case the lack of privacy in between shower / bedroom won't bother you much. In fact you may even realize as the architect did, this is a very efficient use of space. If you're of the former example, the rooftop bar is a nice place you can go in case you need to vacate the room for a little while.
The rooftop bar has monkeys climbing through the walls of plants they have. I think the plants are for the ambiance, no evidence of any carbon credit harvesting schemes here. They also let you smoke up there which is pretty cool, except there are signs that explicitly state that you cannot smoke marijuana.
The same area functions as the breakfast area in the mornings. It gets high points for presentation and the vegan meatballs are 👨‍🍳💋👌. The outside space is also nice, but not usable every day. Rooms also have full height windows, but you may not see so much due to the facade and the fact the property is located within a block of other buildings. The train station side views are nice though. They're also a Hyatt property so count for your +[n]

TLDR: The building suffers from a case of over-inspired architecture resulting in a questionable hard product but I would stay here again. Hyatt affiliation + close to train station + low price override a lack of bathroom walls for me.
I always love a good brauhaus
Finally some sun!
Checks the boxes, service is at the speed you’d expect, food decent but not write it up and tell all of your friends worthy. I’d return.
Rainy day today, they have umbrellas
Great hotel, pick a room on the backside so you have views of the football stadium.
Gyoza are 👨‍🍳🤌! Spot is pretty nice, mixture of indoor and patio tables. Super fast service as well!
The staff here are the best part. We had an issue with our room having no WiFi signal and they were able to get it resolved very fast. The guy who works the pool bar is awesome!
Prices are great, fish was amazing, and the patio is super chill.

Expectations were met. Beds come with a bottle of prosecco so it’s not a bad deal. Food is definitely priced for the location, but if you’re a foreign tourist in these parts it’s about what you’d expect. Met some cool people hanging out there too.
This might be my favorite bar in the city, super chill atmosphere in the morning and great music selection
This place is my fav rooftop in Saranda. The WiFi is fast and it’s not loud so easy place to chill and knock out some emails. Prices are reasonable as well, 300 Ken for a Korca.
Nice enough place, prices reflect the location. Food was good, not overwhelming. Beer selection is limited for an “Irish pub” but it doesn’t detract from the vibe.
Beautiful spot, way more chill than many of the other beach clubs nearby.
They have kittens! The food is great as well. Mosquito repellent is a must have for evening dining in the summer.
2000 for 2 lounges & umbrella, it fills up by around noon. Music is the typical mix of 10 yr old pop w a dj. Extra charge for the pool, u didn’t bother as it was cloudy.
Doner was amazing, fresh, crunchy, salty, all the things you’d want.
Food was fast, prices in line with all the other spots on the promenade, and waiters were prompt.
Spot is perfect, food is great. There isn’t really anything negative you can say about this place.

pin.name: Ingresso dalla strada alla grotta dei pipistrelli

review.text: A bit tricky to find
Cemetery, warm, hot, a bit hard to reach
It’s a fuel station, they sell petrol, not much more to say about it.
Pretty cool little spot, it’s €5 to enter and walk around the complex. There are cats that chill there as well which is a win in my book. Bring a hat and water for summer!

The pros - this property was recently renovated. The fit and finish is quite nice, and a definite improvement with the large showers in the bathroom compared with before. The location is incredibly convenient as well. The staff at the club lounge were great (although an 8 on the survey isn’t good enough for the gym, beware 🙄)

The cons - the rooms do not have a good area to work from. Bummer because the location would be convenient for a business hotel, I just can’t with this one. The regency club also barely qualifies as a lounge, it’s kinda an extended lobby which is just weird.

The 🚩 management pushes a “discretionary service charge” at checkout. This rubs me the wrong way especially as I often am trying to leave at 6am for a flight and am half asleep. Please pay your staff what they deserve instead of pushing annoying American customs.
Typical cityspar experience, basic stuff but not much more
Nice place to sit and have a coffee or beer
Service is meh, but the spot is right
Great place, they won’t judge you stepping off the bus tired, barely coherent, and not speaking Albanian. And, the food was really good.

The spot and service are ok but the food leaves something to be desired if you’re looking for actual sushi. The tataki was cooked completely through and the rainbow roll had similar topped fish which was 70% cooked. If you want fully cooked fish it won’t satisfy, similarly if you want raw fish, it’s not the spot.

Turn & burn, takes along time to get the servers attention. If you solo dine beware they may toss your food. Ordered takeaway and it was leaking instantly so my pants were messed up when I toon a taxi. Location is great, but I probably won’t be back.
The wait staff are great, very attentive. Food is on point and delivered quickly.

Even if you try to speak Serbian you’ll get the RBF attitude from the staff. It’s old, tiny, and tired. The highlights include a friendly stray dog (I gave a star for the dog), they make up for the staff attitude.

It’s a bus station restaurant, that being said it wasn’t bad. My crepes were pleasant enough, cooked fast, and they had free WiFi. Only downside maybe the flies. But that’s a symptom of sitting outdoors & the summer. For note they did accept Mastercard.
Perfect place to stop when you have a red meat deficiency. Meat is on point!
Lovely spot to stop and have a drink or coffee. Even better, they have cats!
It’s a great spot, but tucked away, but worth going off the Main Street to stop and have lunch. I would definitely return
Nice enough place to stop and look around at things. Lots of busses stop here
Nice spot, food is a bit pricey but the location is great.
Spot is nice enough but the prices on the menu are lower than what they charge you and lots of mosquitos in the evening.
Nice spot, a bit of a hike to get there but the extensive collection of artifacts is worth it.
Chill place, sunbeds & an umbrella are 10€
Late and nothing on the bus works. Broken trays, broken seats, broken toilet, and rude staff.
It’s a mall, it’s there
Very rocky, very hot
Food is great, waiters are super nice, and you can’t beat the view
Super nice family run place. The apartments are furnished well, air conditioning was great, and they couldn’t be kinder. I’d definitely stay here again!
Atmosphere here is off the charts. July temps are crazy, but if you sit on the water you can poke your feet into 7c amazingness.
Best restaurant I’ve been to that was located right in a major tourist attraction. The cat that hangs out there is pretty cool as well
Food was great, reasonably priced, and not busy at all. Sit on the terrace for great views. Their cat is also really nice.
Really nice shady spot to duck into, beautiful views on the terrace, only 2bam to enter which is worth it for the photo opp with fewer people than the vista just down the road.

pin.name: City center cats - grandma Ana's cats

review.text: Not so many cats 😢

The good - this property honors marriotts benefits so upgrades to suites are possible without asking. Rooms are spacious and the bathroom was great. The gym is very nice for a hotel as well.

The bad - staff aren’t really on point, you really have to get their attention. The pool/ gym are public and signs say no mobile phone use at the pool. If you’re a gym rat, this is your spot. If you’re looking to cool down, not so much.

The ugly - do not disturb signs mean nothing. At a business hotel I’d expect it to be obvious someone might be on a call. 5 minutes of knocking and phone calls for “room service” which I did not order. The breakfast coffee was also lackluster.

pin.name: Joe's Beach Lounge & Bar Second Location

review.text: Absurdly high prices (€35 for a beach lounge). At least the chairs are nice. Double check the glasses, they were pretty nasty considering the prices they charge - €18.50 for a bottle of water, a cider, and an ice cream. But… it’s the only game at this beach so either pay the price or don’t 🤷‍♂️

pin.name: Botanical Garden of the Faculty of Science, University of Zagreb

review.text: €2 entry is really hard to beat for such a beautiful place to walk around. The variety of fauna is incredible and there is a really cool cat that loves pets 🐱
I love it when a restaurant tells me what to order. Kai fits that bill. Limited menu ✅ (easier to make decisions), super nice staff, kitschy decor. It’s hard to go wrong eating here.
Other than the price being a bit steep, how can you complain? It’s chocolate and ice cream.

It’s a can’t miss for the buildings and because it’s the “thing to do” in Budapest. The indoor pools were dirty and had flotsam so I wasn’t a fan. Outdoor pools were great, downside that the main one is closed for renovations. Discount tix before 9am are the way to go.
Relatively affordable and in the right spot. Definitely a place I’d eat at again!

I would give them 6⭐️ if I could for sheer service and honesty. They have a great selection of taps. The food is heavy (not my speed) but very well prepared. The extra ⭐️? Grabbing takeaway, smells great!
What a pleasant patio to sit for lunch. Plenty of sunlight even in February!

This place is the bomb. Food is super quick, super fresh, and super cheap. It’s got the grease that you need in your life.

Best part is, I know they keep the kitchen super clean! Sunday nights they’ve got the place apart and are bleaching & cleaning everything!!!

pin.name: National Archeological Museum of the Bulgarian Academy of Sciences

review.text: A cold morning here.
Great craft beer bar with a nice selection of rotating taps. From experience what they have changes constantly!
✅ fruit bowl waiting in the room! It’s the small touches that count.
Relaxing afternoon at Continents.
Quaint neighborhood bar. Service is quick and friendly. Food and drinks are top notch.
Great spot to start off Saturday night in Barcelona.
Not open at 4am
Super quick (3-4 min), super fresh (made right then, and super affordable (€4 for a wrap).
Quick service, excellent ice cream. This location really loads up the toppings!

Cool castle, not big and will take less than an hour to see. It’s 2.50€ to get in. If you have to take a bolt to get there, the transport will cost more than admission.

I wouldn’t make a trip out of the way, but if you’re in the area it’s worth the trip.
Quiet sunny morning
Quiet sunny morning
Absolutely delicious. Everything is fresh, they make it right there in front of you!

My food came promptly, was cooked right, and was relatively inexpensive. The outdoor seating has seen better days, but that’s what happens when seats sit outside on a beach 24/7. Also they have a lot of cats

Pleasant spot on the beach with coffee, sandwiches, and other quick items. It’s located right next to the airport which is cool because you can catch the airplanes on final approach. Bakery items are not award winning, but that is my only complaint.
Not super busy tonight
Restaurant is peaceful this morning but lunch has not started yet.
It’s Haagen Dazs, this location is consistent to the brand. The ice cream is a bit overpriced but delicious.
Lots of takeaway action tonight!
Cool market, it’s worth stopping in if you’re close, but there is nothing that warrants a dedicated trip there.
Not too busy at lunchtime
It’s rainy outside today ☔️. But man this place has the (baked) goods! 🍞🥨🥖🥪🫓

Once you get over the fact that it’s a Wendy’s that charges the better part of 20$ (46GEL=$17.13USD) then it isn’t half bad. The food was quick, fried fare you’d expect. The prices are…. Insane. But hey, I was hungry.
Nice selection, staff were really helpful in helping me put together a nice gift for a friend!
Got some awesome Georgian spices for gifts!
Chill evening

59 GEL for 3 people to eat and enjoy wine. The food is absolutely amazing, quick, and delicious.

Cash only!
Not super crowded. Current menu
Really broad selection of taps. Prices are reasonable. 15 lari for a half liter hoegarden pour.

It’s a food court, everyone can eat what they’d like without fighting. So great for groups. Here’s what you can find.
- Irish bar food
- Turkish steak
- coffee & donuts
- sushi, poke, and bao
- beers, drinks, etc
- burgers, sandwiches, and hot dogs
There’s more I’m sure.
Great hard product with the ambiance, patio, and vibe. Soft product in the food needs a bit of improvement. Tough/dry/difficult to eat ribs from my experience.

I was surprised at the low review score and comments. My food came quickly, the chicken was cooked just right (not cheesy or dry, albeit greasy), and the taste was good. However the buffalo did not taste too much like buffalo, more of a spicy chili sauce almost.

Probably my favorite bar in Tbilisi! The selection of craft brews is incredible. 2 size pours (20/40cl) and a dozen or so taps plus multiple fridges with cans. Expect to pay 3-5$ in Lari per pour.

Atmosphere is super laid back, and never too loud.

They also have ping pong, darts, cards, uno, jenga, and more. Ohh, and they’ve got a nice fireplace.

Hip spot for the hipsters. There’s a mix of bars and restaurants along with an industrial feel courtyard furnished with antique bandsaws.

Definitely a spot that gets better the longer the evening drags on.
Great tap selection and very reasonable prices -1.75$-$3.50 for everything they have.
Yes it’s a tourist trap and priced accordingly. However the location is spot on, and a bar in an old tram? Boom I’m sold. 6€ for hot wine is steep, but the atmosphere makes up for it.
Breathtaking statue overlooking the city. You can hike up for free or pay a couple $€£ to ride up in the cable car.
It’s only a few dollars to go up. Bring cash, they do not take cards. The view from the top is stunning.
Absolutely gorgeous church. The gift shop has a very nice selection of icons as well.
The green market here is quite large. Lots of fresh options to choose from.
Be prepared for a food coma. The portions are large and inexpensive. Easily one of the best meals I ate in Georgia!
Really knowledgeable staff and a great place to stop and get off your feet.

pin.name: Cafe Daphna Tbilisi / კაფე დაფნა თბილისი

review.text: The food here is amazing! Prices are very reasonable. You’ll be super full after a meal here.
The Bazar is worth a stop to check out the eclectic things they have on offer. Oh yea, it’s in an old tunnel. That alone is worth the visit.
AMAZING views at sunset. Regardless of your seat location you’ll get easily 180 degrees of Istanbul skyline. The sushi is basic but very good, meat cuts are on point as well.
Sensory overload is the best way to describe the Egyptian bazaar. It’s worth the trip if you’re visiting the grand bazaar nearby. Products are similar as are the vendors.

If you want to sit after a morning of shopping at the Grand Bazaar, you should sit here. The staff are super on the ball, the food is amazing, and the prices are very reasonable. Plus… they have cats hanging out here
Very eclectic store of jewelry, bags, and post cards you won’t find elsewhere in BCN. Mostly line work for the art that was incredibly beautiful.

The ticket includes a small free drink. The boats are basic but clean. The “tour” route is very very short, I kinda wish it went a bit further but the viewpoint of the city is nice from the low boat in the water.
Firmly in the ok category. It’s convenient to the Brandenburgetor, but the servers are rude. They clearly don’t want to be here and give a fair amount of attitude.
The staff working here are really kind. Touristy level prices, but food is served very quick.

I got caught in the rain and this seemed like a good place for a pint and to dry off. The servers are awesome, no crazy upsells, hassle or anything!

Food was delivered quick, was tasty, and they have heat lamps outside. The biggest plus, there are really cool cats that lounge here!

pin.name: Hotel Indigo Berlin - Ku'Damm by IHG

review.text:
The staff working at this Indigo are the best thing about the property. The location is very good with lots of restaurants nearby.

The downside to this property is the desk setup in the rooms. There is no power connection so to work you’ve gotta unplug the fridge or coffee maker. Definitely a big shortcoming for me.
Nice relaxed atmosphere, reasonable prices, and the food is cooked and served very fast.
Very quick service! They had my food out in about 10 minutes flat after I ordered it. Price was very reasonable for Berlin (12-15€/person).

Blink and you may miss the storefront as it’s smaller and inconspicuous.
Prices are low (for Berlin) but the quality was good and came very quickly.
I only ordered a beer and a coffee, but it was good, the service was quick, and the price as ok.
A must see when in Berlin!
Huge selection of Moka pots and other coffee devices. All sizes extra small to 10l.
The staff working here are awesome. Sushi is the most helpful employee here! I bought a really nice pair of shoes as well.
Amazing selection of chocolates and other confectionary! Probably better than most candy stores!
Great doner, convenient location near the train station. Prices are average for Berlin (10€/person ish)

This lounge is firmly in the “I’ll stop in if I get free access” category for me. As a UA Gold member it was free on a LOT economy ticket.

Food was ok, cold cuts served at breakfast. They also had a variety of cookies and biscuits.

I enjoyed eating at Bricks. It was a fancier experience than many similar priced restaurants I dined at in Prague, Expect to pay $15-25 for mains, a bit more if you order the catch of the day or steak.

The servers are fantastic. Ondrej who waited on us gave some of the most attentive service we had in Prague.
This holiday inn is situated convenient to the old town, but far enough go away that it’s quiet. Room rates are reasonable and the staff are attentive. The business center area is great for working.

The food is ok, the quality simply does not compensate for the terrible service. If you make a reservation, my rec is to call and ensure that it's recorded on the date you wish. In my experience, not calling = no dinner.

When you do arrive, the best suggestion I have is to ensure that the waiter reads everything back to you. My question - can I order your suggestion for your favorite dish? - ended with no main meal for me.

I guess the food there isn't that good. I can't really give a description since I had to crib food from the plates of my friends. By the time I stole bites, it was cold.

The bathrooms were very nice.
Bit overpriced but fun. It’s free to go in the gift shop where you can buy the typical chocolate sausage per my (removed by g) photo. Sadly they only offered these small ones in white chocolate.
My goto place to shop when I’m in the neighborhood. They have a broad selection with a proper selection of ingredients to cook from scratch.

We visited on an extremely busy day. The beer we ordered was amazing. U fortunately our food order got lost in the shuffle somehow but the staff were great about it and everything was taken care of promptly.
Beef bar is a great place to overindulge your meat fantasies. Beef is Argentinian/uruguayan, but the cuts are mostly your typical western fare. They’ll even cut you a bigger steak if you ask.
It’s a cool place to get a photo. It’s free to show up. If you want to go inside it’s a paid experience.
It’s free to stop here and has some of the best views of the city. It is a bit of an uphill hike to reach the view, but totally worth the effort.

Very convenient spot to have a sit, use the bathroom, and have some refreshments after exploring Prague Castle.

Prices are not low, but fair. Expect to spend about 3-4€ for a Pilsner. All that while looking down on one of the prettiest European capitals.

Great service, the restaurant was packed on Christmas but they were able to seat us outside sans reservation. Overall it’s a really nice spot, while chilly outside they have heaters and blankets. Our waiter Dominic was on the spot with everything.
Very cool tower, free to walk by!
Reasonably priced sandwiches made incredibly quick. It’s conveniently located near all the main touristy things so a good place to pin if you’re on a budget.

I did not get a chance to eat, we walked in understanding that may not be a possibility. Waiters have a canned English response and simply didn’t take a second to answer a question about a future reservation. Guess I’ll never get a chance to sample the food
The staff working here are super friendly. It’s super convenient to all the tourist sites as well

Pretty bad even for airport food. 5€ for a tiny orange juice is high even for airports. My hot sandwich came cold and with stale bread. There aren’t great food options in this airport but this is really bad.

pin.name: Nobu Hotel Marrakech, an SLH Hotel

review.text: This hotel is amazing. The interior is tastefully decorated, rooms are large & modern, and it’s conveniently located a 10 minute walk from the Medina.

A peaceful oasis in the middle of the crazy winding streets. The ambiance is ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️. Cool temperature in the gardens even in the heat of the day, and tranquility with birds chirping lightly in the background.

Very convenient to the metro and tram and the right mix of shops for day to day shopping. The food court has a broad variety, shops are not “luxury brands” but rather include stores like Al Campo, intimissi, etc
The best meal of my week was served here.

The rooms at this hotel have definitely seen better days. The courtyards, lobby, grounds, and pool are stunning. Beyond that the property is in need of a renovation. My room had wires falling out of the trim, an old stained shower curtain, and a sliding outside door that has not really lockable (although it was on a high floor so no one could easily get in).
Selection is a bit limited beyond booze and cleaning supplies. They have a nice selection of drinks though.
This chilis has great staff, they’re always kind, super polite, and the service is quick. This location has a nice outdoor patio shared with papa John’s. It’s typically fairly empty during the day.

Variety to the max! Take a carriage ride, see snake charmers, enjoy a tea, then top it off with a fresh squeezed juice!

Be prepared to haggle, many stalls do not have marked prices.
One of the “must see spots” when you visit Marrakech. Go early to enjoy the gardens before it gets crowded!
Classy place, it tends to be pretty empty during the evenings. Beers run 6-7$, cocktails about $10. They have live music some nights which is pretty cool as well.

€42.50 to enter is a bit steep for this lounge. The food selection is limited and some items were stale. I probably would not pay to get in, but if you have free access the internet is ok, the food is free, and they have a nice selection of cold drinks in the case.
Kiosko in Mercat Boqueria. I sat here because it was empty. The food was a tad salty, although came quickly. Prices were reasonable.
Selection of artistic socks many with food themes. They also sell a variety of jewelry including stainless. The shirts are the coolest thing they offer, super funk Hawaiian designs.
I like the creative concept. Everything is 10€. Mostly shirts,scarves, and small purses. Ladies apparel only. There’s a good place in the street for men to stand outside and look at their phones.

This location of vodafone does not have fast or attentive service, they also seem to have a very small amount of stock. It took 2 return trips for 3 total trip to finalize minor account changes as they did no have sims in stock and they did not restock on time.
They have a dozen plus taps. The food is typical Spanish pub fare, but good, and served quickly. The location is convenient right on la rambla.

This Lufthansa lounge is smaller, but very has the amenities of a larger lounge. The food spread is German and heavy, but felt refreshing after eating airplane food for a day. The shower area is particularly nice but there can be a line sometimes.
Amazing trendy spot for a quick brunch. The waffle fries are so crispy that they are almost potato chips!

Fresh food, quick service, and spacious patio! The food and decor are fresh with no frills. Service is fast and pleasant. The bread for the banh mi is just as a banh mi should be -> crisp on the outside, fluffy and soft within.
Cool spot buried deep in the depths of BCN. Prices were reasonable and the clothing quality is high. It’s a bit tricky to find as there is not much street signage nor identifying marks.
Cute little outdoor space that serves sandwiches and non alcoholic drinks. Quick service, midrange prices.
Stunning pastry designs mimicking life. The prices are subjectively high, but objectively low when you consider the sheer artwork that goes into the creation. They taste really good too 😎!
The servers are friendlier than other bars in the area.
Very cool spot with crazy art you can purchase. Prices are reasonable (100€ for many large prints).
Quick food, fresh ingredients, and Vietnamesesque flavors mixed with a lot of other southeast Asian spice affinity. They serve a limited brunch menu in the mornings.
See the penguins and stop in here for lunch.
Great food, service with a smile. I’ve pinned it to my list.
Decent for an airport
Decent place for a round of drinks with friends

pin.name: DIGITALMOBILE2 Reparacion de Telefonos Moviles / Mobile Phone repair /SIM cards

review.text: Quick to put on a screen protector and reasonably priced (7€).
It’s a pretty cool place to see. Literally the “must see” location in BCN. Buy tickets online, it’s your best option.
This place is a zoo, but it’s not the fault of the employees. Any rental car location with lots of out of town drivers heading to international flights will be a zoo.
It’s an airport. Lots of companies fly here. They can take you anywhere in the world you’d like to go in a metal tube.
The service is slow, but if you need a cable or cell phone in person they will have it. They also sell appliances and e-bikes.

This place is one of the few where you won’t get kicked out for writing on the walls. Bring a nice wide tip like a sharpie brand to get the best messaging adherence.

The food is also very good. I’d suggest the ceviche.
Nice selection, reasonable prices.

pin.name: ALITRASTERO | Trasteros en el centro de Alicante

review.text: Nice enough place. I stored my bike here for a few months, it worked out ok!
Great place for a quick beer and fast bites.
Nice selection of the bigger teams as well as a wide variety of Spanish ones. They also have a great shoe selection.
Good cuts, good prices.
Staff working here are great and there usually is no more than a 5 minute wait.
Reasonable prices and the laundry was done quickly. What more can you want?

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express & Suites Playa del Carmen by IHG

review.text:
Great location a 2 minute walk from 5th. It's far enough away you won't be bothered by noise. The walls in some units are not the thickest so it's not uncommon to hear neighbors. There are many airbnb units here, some share balconies, others do not.

The biggest downside right now is the rooftop pool being closed (November 2023).
Chill local bar atmosphere. The pasta dishes while pricey for Spain at €15 are well worth the money in flavor.

pin.name: Hilton Grand Vacations Club Paradise Las Vegas

review.text:
The upside: this location is less expensive than staying on the strip, is quiet, and has very nice views.

The downside: checkout time is early (10am), it’s a hike from the strip, and it’s not the flashiest hotel.
Decent empanadas. Not amazing.
Great cut, reasonable price.
Nice spot with great beers. What more can you want? They’ve got a patio so you can bring your hound.
Food is amazing! Waiters speak many languages (mandarin, English, and Spanish at least) and the service was excellent.
Perfectly competent lounge. It’s a smaller space so options are limited. Staff were downright nice for NY!

Thr best part of this restaurant is the bats that appear at sunset, The target crowd is older (40s+) and the prices are what you would expect in the states, but overall the experience and food are good.
BA quality milanesa. Bring mosquito spray if you visit in the evenings. They get thick and are relentless.

pin.name: Guy Fieri's American Kitchen Bar Cancún

review.text: It’s about what you would expect from a tv personality plastering their name all over an airport restaurant. The real crime is my card being charged in USD at an extortionate rate.

A trip to Aquila is one of the “Must dos” when you visit Cape Town! Yes, you’ll have to wake up early and drive 2 hours to make the day trip. The experience is worth every sleepy moment.

Activities such as quad bikes and horseback riding cost extra but are competitively priced. Day trips include access to a buffet lunch and breakfast. It’s your typical hospital or cruise ship quality provided in Asian, British, and Local flavors.

Bring a swimsuit if you wanna take a dip in the (heated) pool. You can also just lay back on the lawn and drink beers or take a nap. My main regret is not staying overnight!

pin.name: Holiday Inn Savannah Historic District by IHG

review.text:
The property is ok, it isn’t really “bad” after all. Unfortunately there’s nothing really special, however it’s priced like it might be. You’ll pay 5* property prices for things like valet service.

The real problem is, they don’t really offer 5* service. You can’t for instance… ask for your car to be ready at a certain time the day before. It’s afterall a Holiday Inn.
My fav mall in BKK!

Karibu specializes in game, expect typical steakhouse dishes but from springbok, ostrich, etc instead of beef (although they have beef/chicken as well). IMO this is the best place for lunch or dinner in the wharf!
Quaint coffee shop, the people running it are super welcoming and kind. The coffee is great too!
Very cool place to find Knick knacks, clothing, and souvenirs. Prices are a bit high, but the stores are unique!
Chill spot on the beach with laid back vibes. Portions are very large. Views are amazing.
Nice little spot on the beach. Prices are reasonable, water is great!
Enjoy the beach, then walk a block and enjoy a steak!
Great spot with fast WiFi and large portions. They’ve got floating cushions in the water, a variety of lounges, and a nice pool.
Great pizza sauce. That’s probably the most important thing. It’s a nice space is a close second. Great beach views, a nice pool nearby. What’s not to like?
You can always find what you need at tops!
A very chill place to have a beer and relax. The ocean view is incredible. Sit back and relax and the breeze cools you down just enough in the shade.
Great location close to the beach. Rooms are spacious and inexpensive for what they are.
This place is the quintessential beach shack in the perfect way. Relax and order a large meal. The views are worth the price of admission alone.
I did a day pass to enjoy the pool and drink some beers here. Great place to do exactly that:

Make reservations, the best spots will be taken if you don’t even if you are early. Food prices are on the high side for Thailand in general but a budget for Samui. The sunsets are amazing when the conditions are right! Be prepared w your camera!
My favorite place to eat in central world! It’s tucked away upstairs and tends to be quieter than the other food hall. Bring cash!

pin.name: Sindhorn Midtown Hotel Bangkok, Vignette Collection by IHG

review.text:
The Sindhorn Midtown is a brand new property. Rooms are spacious and have great views of the other tall residential buildings. The pool is set on the 18th floor and is absolutely breathtaking.

The best part about this property is the availability of food. The breakfast spread rivals any other hotel in Bangkok. There’s a cafe open until 10 with incredibly reasonable prices considering the price point of the rooms. Plus, there’s an adjacent 24 hour 711!
Chic & instagrammable. Prices are a bit higher than other nearby coffee shops, but the experience is worth it.

The tallest building in Vietnam is also the best hotel in the entire country.

Where to begin? By default you’ll get a high floor room because the hotel is in the tallest part of the Landmark Tower. Breakfast is incredible so make sure you check the option when booking. The pool view is amazing.

It’s a bit pricey for hotels in Vietnam, but sets a high bar and should put other 5* properties on notice.
Make a reservation, it’s crowded

The newer Pasteur Street is a way better location than the OG. It’s a corner open layout. Order an amazing beer and watch the world go by. If you’re with others who like to shop, send them to Ben Thanh and enjoy spending your money in a way less hectic environment.
Peaceful park to lay out and enjoy your time in HCMC.

If you want the Insta Vibes this is your place. Air con on the ground floor and a great rooftop with fans. The food is really good as well, but that’s an afterthought as your stuntin for the gram right?

pin.name: BELGO TON THAT THIEP - BELGIAN CRAFT BEER & RESTAURANT

review.text:
If you like Belgian style beers, you will be in for a treat. Honestly, I can’t tell the difference between their brews and the stuff I can buy at home. Add in amazing fare and you have a winner.

Bottles are available for takeaway so you can bring a couple home with you. You’ll thank yourself when you return.
It’s the OG HCMC location, you can’t go wrong. Sure, this tap room isn’t as nice as newer locations. But, the beer is still cold & delicious.

IMO the best craft brews in HCMC (which is an amazing beer city!) they’ve always got 🔥 rotational taps. The food is also great. Stop in for the tomahawk special Sunday for 999k vnd ($40ish usd), you won’t be disappointed!
The sushi is very reasonably priced, service was great! They also have hibachi tables available with a nice selection of meat options.

pin.name: John Pennekamp Coral Reef State Park Visitor Center

review.text: The visitor center is not very big, but tells the story of the park. It also has air conditioned restrooms!!! It’s worth the 10 minutes to see if you’re already here!
Fun little part of Singapore to check out. There are sellers with a variety of goods including cool things like old coins and stamps.
Great location!
Meh, it’s Miami so there’s a ton of good food. I would not have eaten here if it were not in the hotel. Service was very slow and surly. Prices were definitely high for the spread.

Bayside Marketplace is 100% a tourist trap. That doesn’t mean you shouldn’t go. It’s a fun place to took around, have a drink, and people watch. Tons of shopping and many restaurants. If you’re feeling adventurous, take a jet boat ride!
Fun little museum tucked in next to Marina Bay Sands. Tucked in is an understatement, realistically the building is part of singapores skyline!
It’s a tourist trap, but an amazing one. Bring cash, be prepared to haggle over prices. You’ll find a ton of shirts, coffee, bags, souvenirs, and more.
They have cookies, they’re available, and you can purchase them. They also sell candy which is cool.

Gunung Mulu is a bucket list item for anyone who loves bats. They’re everywhere, millions of them!
The park is remote, even for asia: expect to spend over a day in transit each way if you are coming from the west.
Park fees are incredibly reasonable, as are the tours. Book in advance as they are space limited and can sell out.
Small but efficient airport. Realistically due to its size you only need to show up about an hour before departure.

pin.name: InterContinental Singapore Robertson Quay by IHG

review.text:
“The ultimate value” in Singapore. Rooms are 200$ish a night but cheaper compared with the other 5* hotels. It’s filled with business travelers so it’ll be quiet at night.

Great hotel that is modern and well laid out. Rooms are small, but it’s Singapore.

If you visit Mulu, your hotel options are limited. This one is by far the nicest. Food options are limited, but decent. The pool could be cleaner, but you don’t visit this property for the pool, you go to see the park. I’m glad I came!
Great place to grab a bite.
One of my fav hawker spots to eat in Singapore. There’s something for everyone to eat here.
Nice stop for a bite on the way to the islands. Bring cash!
The gardens are amazing, best of all it’s free to walk around! Can’t miss stop in Singapore!

pin.name: Jesselton Point Ferry Terminal - South Jetty

review.text: The best thing to do in KK is to take a trip to the islands. It all starts here. Bring what you need for the day and cash, then get some tickets and hop on a boat!
Smaller airport, security is pretty quick. There are very few food options.
It’s easy to see this cave if you’re headed to deer cave, it’s only a few meters away. It’s smaller, but has a neat entrance. Buy your tickets in advance, they sell out!
One of the coolest parts of Mulu! A building could literally fit inside. Millions of bats to see, bring good shoes to walk, there’s lots of guano and humidity around.
This jetty is not falling down. Bring shoes, you’ll want them to walk. In all seriousness as you get off the boat look down. There are tons of cool fish to see here!
It’s the bucket list property to stay at in Singapore for a reason! Non hotel guests can access the observation deck up top, but you’ll have to stay to enjoy the amazing pool.

The Hyatt Regency KK is an older property that looks a bit dated but is still well kept. The club is nothing to right home about, but offers the typical small breakfast spread and tea in the afternoon.

If you are in Kota looking for a beach club, Breeze may pop up on your list. If you want my TLDR - it’s not actually a beach club, simply a hotel pool bar. It’s not a bad establishment per se, but google maps may mislead you.

It’s obvious from the map that this place is part of a hotel. No big deal, many beach clubs are. However it isn’t really setup to cater to non hotel guests. Staff may be a bit clueless when you ask - is the pool and beach club open to non hotel guests? I asked at the front and was told it was. However you can’t use the pool (ok with me, I just want to read a book and drink beer 🤣). Nor is your chair entitled to a towel so bring your own (if that’s allowed).

Overall I can’t fault the staff. Who I can fault is owenership of the property who is trying to market Breeze as a standalone beach club with separate google listing. It’s a hotel pool, why advertise it as separate unless it’s an establishment that truly stands on its own? I’ve been to many beach clubs attached to hotels, this one just isn’t really a beach club.

Edit. Don’t bother ordering mixed drinks, really not good at all.
Decent bar with lots of tvs! The ladies working the bar seem nice. They are bilingual as well which helps a lot!
Cash only, but very reasonable prices. Don’t expect a large steak in your bun for 12 sgd, but the food is delicious and the price right.
Relaxed place on the water to have seafood. What more could you want?

pin.name: Hotel Indigo Miami Brickell by IHG

review.text:
The Indigo is one of the less expensive properties in Brickell. You'll pay roughly 30% less to stay here all for the price of walking a single extra block or two to get to your hotel. Overall the hotel has large rooms, is tastefully furnished, and the staff are excellent.

The downside is that there are some resident homeless people on that walk back. I never had any issues, but it may deter some people.

This Intercontinental is in the right location, and is the right price for a 5* hotel. Here's what you will want to know:
1. Breakfast is expensive, expect to spend about 50$/ person if you don't add it to your room rate.
2. Booking "Club rooms" at this property is useless. The club is closed so you only get a higher floor.
3. The Air Conditioning is frigid. There is no way to get a room above about 70F. Turning off the AC will still leave the room very very cold.
4. The pool is having a renovation done. Don't expect a great place to hang out. It was loud when I visited and the views were subpar.

This lounge is a bit crowded, lacks showers, but is overall a good place to relax before your flight. Expect to spend $59/ person if you need to buy access. Free with a United Club Card or Lounge Membership from United. It's also free if you're SA Gold in some limited cases

It’s wendys, you’re going for mediocre fast food in the first place. If you only have an hour for lunch, expect to spend half of it waiting in line. Trying to order ahead won’t help, you’ll just end up waiting longer than people who ordered in the restaurant who showed up after you. The staff seem about as happy as you will be after eating here.
Amazing place to work

This is an older and dated property. There are tons of Marriotts available in KL, so you're probably considering the Sheraton based on price. It's not a bad hotel per se, but be prepared for the following:
1. the rooms are definitely dated, you will have some peeling paint, worn out seals on shower doors, etc.
2. The cleanliness level was not terrible, but not high. There were definitely long hairs left in the shower/sink before I checked in.
3. For younger guests who have status or are planning to book a club access room: expect to be held to a much higher standard than older guests. You'll see plenty of people with grey hair wearing shorts & open toed shoes. You will 100% be asked to change if you wear something similar.
There are cats! Lots of them!!! If you have 100THB, love cats, and want to chill in the ac, this place will be your jam!

pin.name: Hotel Indigo Phuket Patong by IHG

review.text:
One of the nicest properties in Patong. The Hotel Indigo is close enough that you can walk to anything, but a couple blocks removed from the noisiest spots so it’s quiet at night.

The property features 2 pools, at least 6 restaurants physically attached to the same building, and… a 24 hr 7/11 with coffee bar. Truly what more can you want?

Kudo is a really chill spot to hang out on the beach and enjoy a meal. Prices are higher than most of the nearby restaurants in Patong. However this nearby restaurants do not have a patio that is literally open to the beach.

Food is a mixture of popular Thai & Western dishes. Dishes range between $10-15 and drinks from $4-7.
My favorite mall in BKK! Head up to the top floor for amazing “street food” stalls. You’ll find several Michelin Bib Gourmand options to choose from.
Amazing place to grab a beer and chill with friends! There is a wide variety of food options so everyone can pick what they want. Prices are western, but not exorbitant.

I love enjoying a western treat every once and awhile in Bangkok. Fowlmouth is one of those treats. Grab a beer and find a seat in the commons while you wait, then be ready for the best Nashville hot chicken in Thailand!

It’s a lot of fun to hang out around Khao San. The action does not get going until after dark, it continues well past midnight. There are plenty of taxis available to take party goers back to their hotel if they are not staying close.

THERE ARE CATS!!!!

Go to the back of the park away from the bts and turn left towards the office. Bring food and enjoy play time with the kittens!!!
A cool place to buy some sweet kicks for very low price!

The intercontinental Pattaya is one of my favorite properties to relax by the sea. Great location a 20 min walk or 5 min ride from the walking street.

Opt for the breakfast option, you won’t be disappointed with the spread. Even better go for a club suite which includes afternoon tea and cocktails as well.
Quick, affordable, and delicious.
Chill atmosphere and great music on the weekends.
One of the coolest spots for coffee in Ko Kret. After you get your drink head inside and veer right to sit at the bar overlooking the water. Be careful not to drop anything!
Hip spot where you can get some nice shots for the gram. The real bonus however is that they have cats!!!
This is the spot for craft beer in Bangkok. There is a broad variety of taps, nice music, and plenty to eat.

pin.name: Staybridge Suites Bangkok Thonglor, an IHG Hotel

review.text:
The location is incredibly convenient, a 10 minute walk to the BTS and all sorts of food and stores around. The neighborhood is very wholesome with lots of families around.

Rooms are large, well lit, and have a small kitchen/washer to keep fresh. If you’re visiting from a cooler climate the washer is a lifesaver as you may sweat through your clothes more often here.

pin.name: Pad Thai Kratong Thong by ama

review.text:
If you are touristing and visit Wat Pho you will end up hot, tired, and hungry. Pad Thai Kratong Thong is directly across from the main entrance. You’ll be out of the heat enjoying a delicious meal in no time.

Chic ambiance, open air, and relatively inexpensive.
Alicante is small, so are it’s malls. It’s not a bad place, but selection is a bit limited. The alcampo on the ground floor is nice.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express Bangkok Soi Soonvijai, an IHG Hotel

review.text:
“Location” rating is subjective. If you are visiting Bangkok as a tourist, this property will be a bit far from the things you want to see. If you’re visiting for the hospital, you are in the right hotel.

Staff are lovely, the rooms decent, and the breakfast is quite good for a HIE. They have a handful of small restaurants/kiosks on the property outside which were decent as well.
Great air conditioned respite from the heat. Drinks are made quickly with 💕!

It’s the OG must see indoor shopping experience in Bangkok. There are newer, bigger, and more modern malls, but they don’t have the same old school charm. If you see the whole place, it’ll take a few hours, wear good shoes.
Prices are reasonable and food comes quickly because you can just get it off the conveyor. Is it the best quality? No. Can you eat a full meal for under $10 and feel good about it? Yes.
Located right next to self check at Alcampo
Absolutely amazing, even if you visit on a rainy day like I did.
You are not coming here for fine dining. Cheap beers, a nice spot, what’s not to like?
A bit more expensive than other stores in the area, but the selection is nice.
Food was as requested, staff were lovely.
Prices are a bit high but the flavors are on point.
Amazing, there is literally no other way to sum up how good this place is. The sashim sampler was huge and relatively inexpensive (65€ with a couple of drinks).
A rocky beach with picturesque and crystal clear water. It will be way less crowded than other beaches in the city, but often has people sunbathing nude nearby.
Paya del Postiguet will be crowded on the best days, so get there early to get a prime spot.
The food at Island Grill is decent, but like the rest of The Keys these days, far overpriced. Bring mosquito spray if you dine in the evening and want to sit outside, they can get incredibly thick.
Flavors were on point, beers were cold & large, and the view outside is incredible. My steak was a bit on the tough side, and the side of potatoes were small but overall the meal was great.
Amazing steaks! I think that’s all you need to say in a review about an Argentinian Grill no? The 500g did not disappoint!
If you are looking for a relatively inexpensive plate of greasy churros with an amazing view of the sea, this place is for you. Service can be a bit slow, so be prepared to wait.

The staff are amazing and will go out of your way to treat you well. I came 15 minutes before closing and while the kitchen was out of what I wanted to eat (lots of nigiri) they still made some great suggestions and it all turned out amazing!

Some minor gripes that don’t detract in the star count: it’s really bright inside 🤣. it’s also a bit on the pricey side for Alicante. Overall it isn’t enough to dock it with how great the employees are.

Enjoy amazing views of a breathtaking beach while you have a meal. Expect typical spanish fare including hot/cold seafood based tapas and a selection of rice dishes (which are ordered per person, min 2).

Make reservations if you want the be seated in the main area on popular days. The restaurant can be crowded.
Counter service fast casual chain that specializes in wraps, tacos, and bowls. Flavor and spice profile are on point but possibly a bit salty.

Small laundromat, but it has everything you’ll need. Soap is included in the price of the machines. 10-16€ to wash & dry, coin only or you can pay w card via an app. There is a small machine that changes €5 &€10 bills.
A great place to have a relaxed meal. Not fast by any means, but worth waiting for.

Ziryab is an amazing flavor fusion experience. You have the option of ordering a set of tapas or off an executive menu. We tried both and everything was spectacular.

The space is small and it’s located in a touristy area. Best to get a reservation if you plan to dine on a weekend or popular evening.

If you want a mouth watering buttery steak Bar Tomeu is your jam. It’s a small location with a handful of tables inside and out and a relatively subdued atmosphere. Prices are reasonable (17-25€ for meat dishes) and portions are somewhat large.
Quiet atmosphere with prompt service and nice presentation. The sushi is decent but not life changing.

This Caprobo is relatively large for a Spanish grocery store and has a wide variety of options, but limited selection of each. It's a very convenient location for those staying next door at the Sabatic Sitges. You can find medicines, sundries, and things beyond basic groceries, although there is not a large selection of prepared foods.

This bakery is a small location, but they have a relatively large selection. Sweet and savory pastries along with bread and a drink cooler are the offering. Prices are not low, but not high either. The location is often busy with a line out the door, but the service is quick enough.

This place has the meats. It’s more expensive than similar shops in Cordoba, and it’s smaller, but they have a very nice selection. Stop in here especially if you’re looking for dry aged, they have you covered.

Cool place for tourists, but probably not a great choice to grab a cup on your way to work. No large sizes and prices are high. The experience is phenomenal and it’s worth stopping in to enjoy the ambiance and check out all the roast art stuff.

The pros: rooms are very very big for NY. The hotel is centrally located and right next to grand central so very convenient to getting around.

The cons: there is a large amount of wear and tear on the property. The climate control may not work super well and the room was a bit cool overnight in the winter.

Overall a nice property at a good price in the right place.

In many cities, Yard House is comparatively pricey. In the middle of Times Square, it’s a bargain. Dozens of beers on tap, plenty of sports to watch and Americanized “international” fare such as poke nachos (prob my fav). If everyone orders the same streak at your table remember to check the done-ness indicator, we ended up w the wrong ones, but they were still amazing. Overall a great place to chill w friends, get drunk, watch sports, and shoot the shit.

The staff at the hotel are lovely, but there are some major inconveniencing factors. Primary to those is this is a business hotel and the WiFi does not work in all the rooms. I stayed for a day on a layover to get some work done. I was on my hotspot the entire time or working from the bar. This is simply unacceptable for a brand such as intercontinental.
Very cool bar. If you're into Instagrammable spots, this place will be your jam. If you're a foreigner bring cash, this was one of the few places in Buenos Aires that had trouble processing my card.
Definitely one of the most fun things to do in Montevideo on a Sunday! Go early, it can get a bit crowded by noon. Bring cash for most of the vendors!

The Aloft Montevideo is in the right place in Montevideo. It is across the street from a shopping mall, a block away from starbucks, and surrounded by some of the best restaurants in the city.

Overall, the staff are friendly, rooms are spacious, and many have great views. Some cons (that do not detract from the 5* rating) include bathroom soaps that come out of a dispenser and don't work well and very expensive beers in the bar.

Many of the steakhouses in Montevideo are jam packed on Saturdays and Sundays. This is not the case for Uruguay National Parilla Gourmet. They have an adequate number of servers as well meaning that you won't have to wait 20 minutes for the check. Overall the food is good and the restaurant is a nice family atmosphere for a meal.

If you expect a trendy bodega atmosphere and are not in a rush, Niño Gordo is amazing. The food is great, but block two hours on your calendar for dinner at least. Expect prices to be high for Buenos Aires, but the flavors are on point.

Don’t be surprised by a quick appetizer, the main course may come an hour later. It’s best to savor the dishes due to the pause in between. For those paying with foreign cards, be sure to bring spot cash: service is not included and cannot be paid via card.

A really fun place to get out of the city and check out on the weekends! You can take the train, an Uber (approximately $10 from the capital), or drive to get there.

The Puerto is made up of various kiosks and restaurants in a spread out mall like fashion. Lots of fun things to choose from, but bring cash as many vendors only accept cash/debit cards.

Large portions and cooked extremely well. My bondiola was done just right. The restaurant patio is spacious, but almost set in a parking lot so it can be quite hot even in the shade during the summer. Plus points that they accept credit cards, however there is a 10% surcharge for using them.

The Renaissance São Paulo is older construction, but mostly well maintained. The decor and furnishings are modern enough that you won’t notice the property’s age at first glance.

The good: incredibly helpful staff. The bellboy brought my Rappi order up when the elevator took too long to reach me. The club lounge has amazing views and is not too crowded nor full of out of control children. Rooms are spacious and comfortable.

Things that could be better: club breakfast is definitely on the greasier side of things. The walls are incredibly thin and you will hear your neighbors singing at 2am should they choose to do that.

If I had one word to describe Cerezo, I would say it’s “boutique.” The fish is high quality, and while not cheap it is by no means expensive. Cash/debit cards are best, you can use a credit card but will pay a 10% surcharge.

Amazing sandwiches! Expect to gain a kg over lunch as you inevitably overeat and order more than 1. Don't overlook the fries. Extra crispy and fried with rosemary, it would probably be sacrilegious to eat them with a sauce at all.
PS they do delivery over Rappi!

You have to visit if you’re in Rio, and it’s amazing enough to deserve 5 stars. My review is more of the small tactical things that may make your visit better:

Arrive early. Before 8:30 early. I booked the 7:20 am train departure, but ended up going up the hill at 7:40. By the time I was leaving at 9am the line was well out to the street and trains going up the hill were standing room only.

Bring a hat, the direct sun can be brutal! Bring water as well. If you go early beware the cafe is not open until 9am.

Overall be prepared for the lines and rude people going to get shots for the Gram.

This Grand Hyatt is an amazing contemporary property that I would definitely stay at again. I’ll start with the pros: the rooms are modern, well laid out, filled with light, and have spacious balconies. The toiletries are also a notch above the small average soaps you’ll get in most hotels.

If you’re a beachgoer who just wants to relax, this is the property for you. Located right across the street from beautiful white sand beaches you can simply wake up and enjoy the sun lounging all day then retire for room service or sushi at the in hotel restaurant which received a Michelin Star.

The complaints I have were very not picky: the buttons to call a waiter around the pool do not work, expect to flag a staff member down. Pool food is also ok, not great like the prices imply. The club lounge is crowded and the children can be noisy/ running around. All in all minor annoyances that did not detract from my 2+ week stay.

The food is good, but not amazing. I ordered a filet cooked medium rare and it came medium well with extra char on the outside. The flavor was still good, but it was difficult to cut with the especially dull steak knives provided.

The food alone would earn this restaurant 4 stars in my book, but the service was not great. I paid, went to finish the last couple ounces of beer and it was almost taken out of my hand. We spent no more than 30 minutes in the restaurant and it felt like we were being hurried out the whole time. Very surreal experience for a restaurant that was mostly empty on a Saturday night.

Perfect hotel for a family holiday to Rio. The property features an amazing pool grounds (hot tub, children's pool, family pool) with live grilling every day and several restaurants adjacent. The location is relatively close to Ipanama and Copacabana, but not close enough that you can easily walk. Luckily Ubers are abundant and the hotel has hire cars as well.

Now for the bad: the property is an older style building with low ceilings. I stayed in two different rooms during my stay and the latter one had a terrible mildew smell in the hallway.
