The property is LOUD. I stayed over New Years and there were two days of private parties that lasted till at least 3am and literally vibrated the windows. Don't bother staying here if the noise isn't for you.

pin.name: Botanical Garden of Rio de Janeiro

review.text: Simply breathtaking! The Rio Botanic Gardens are a must see for plant lovers, bird watchers, and anyone who just wants to enjoy nature in the heart of Rio. Be forewarned, tickets at the park are CASH only so order tickets online if you wish to pay with a card.

Amazing does not even come close to describing how good The Box Sushi is. I ordered two of the chef's samplers that came with 6 pieces each. It was so good and while I was full, I requested the waiter bring me another 6 pieces but to surprise me. I was not disappointed.

The chefs work magic in this kitchen. The location is low-key and unassuming on a residential street in Ipanama. Sit outside and enjoy the amazing weather in Rio while you wait for the magic to arrive. Prices are very reasonable (approximately $40 usd for 18 pieces & 2 beers). I will be returning in the future!

This property scores average for an intercontinental. The staff are lovely, from checkin to departure they try really hard. The hotel is located conveniently centric between Casio Viejo, shopping, and other points of interest. On the other hand, you’ll be driving everywhere.

The breakfast is decent, but not award winning. The pool area is nice, but the pool is in need of repairs on the bottom, and they could do with a bar. Rooms are a bit tired, but overall ok.

Things I really disliked: the club is only open for very abbreviated hours, you’re not going to use it as a spare work space. The bathrooms have very low water pressure. There is no minibar/fridge in the room? Seriously what is that. If you want to chill drinks you have to order ice and that takes about an hour on average.

Overall it’s an ok stay, I’d probably check out a new property next time I’m in Panama.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express Bogota - Parque La 93

review.text:
This Holiday Inn is very conveniently located. You can feel relatively safe to walk out the door and grab something to eat. Based on the location alone, I’d definitely stay again.

There are 2 things that lead me to dock a star. 1. The blinds were broken so I couldn’t open the windows at all. 2 the bathroom was not the cleanest - there was mold on the ceiling and soap scum on the floor of the shower when I checked in.

This is hands down the best sushi I’ve eaten in Panama, and overall is up there in terms of some of the better sushi Ive had in the world. It was not a “top 10” plate in my lifetime, but the 12 pc sushi sampler got pretty close.

For the price, the service could definitely be better. You really do have to hound the staff to take your order. Maybe I just went on an off day. Overall, I’ll probably give it another try one day.

If seeing the Panama Canal is on your bucket list, this is the company to do it with. Book direct for the best price. They’ll pick you up at your hotel early.

Breakfast/ lunch were not Michelin Star meals, but were not bad. Bring cash for snacks/ beers if that’s your jam.

pin.name: InterContinental Movich Medellín, an IHG Hotel

review.text:
I have deeply conflicted opinions about this hotel. At times I feel it could be a 5 star experience, then there are 2-3 star moments.

I’m start at the top. It’s a 5 star luxury hotel, restaurants, bars, conference rooms, pool, rec area. It checks all the boxes. The staff do go out of their way as well. Case in point, on my birthday they delivered a cake and fruit plate to my room, no request, just out of that blue.

On the other hand, some of the groups staying here can get rowdy for a business hotel. Think mariachi band at 10pm that you can hear on the top floor. It’s South America so I can’t say I was surprised about spotty WiFi. I was surprised there was a full day with almost no service (speed tests were showing 0mbps up/down throughout the day. That’s simply unacceptable. Luckily this only happened 1/7 days I stayed, but this killed my productivity.

Overall it was a good property, albeit not great.
Cool outdoor spot with amazing swinging chairs at the bar. Come for sunset and sit on the water side for amazing fiery color and reflections off the bay.

Benihana multi plaza is just an example of a bad restaurant. The sushi rice is cooked without any seasoning so was beyond bland. The fish was cut in a weird way making it hard to eat, and did not seem the freshest.

Sushi on top of iceberg lettuce with lime? Just odd. I never got the soup or salad that was supposed to be included in my meal. Overall just a bad experience.
This place has the beers. All of them. I don’t think I’ve ever seen a better selection of both variety and quality in Central America. Belgians, American beers, niche, you name it.

This is the spot for brunch in Poblado. Overall the vibe is laid back and has general patio space with tons of plants around. Don’t like breakfast food -> no problem: they serve regular meals all day. Don’t like beer -> amazing craft cocktails. Food is on point as well.

pin.name: COLOMBIA CRAFT BREWING COMPANY - Cerveza Artesanal / Craft Beer

review.text: Low key place to grab a pint or two. Prices are low, even for Colombia. The staff are amazing. Beer quality is great, but not the best I’ve ever had. The latter fact still doesn’t deter me from giving a full 5* rating.
It’s hooters, you know what to expect when you go here. Staff are a bit uptight, but they’ve got lots of tvs with all the games on. It’s not great, but it’s ok.

This place sucks is the understatement of the century. I’m a Globalist and this gives Hyatt a black eye in my book.

To start with: don’t expect the hotel to actually book any amenities or add-ons you pay for in advance. I booked spa services and had a confirmation but the hotel had 0 record of them. Next, expect an old dingy room where things probably won’t work. My experience: no water in the shower, missing faucet handles, and mold everywhere.

If the hotel advertises special events, seriously stay away, they aren’t actually real. Case in point: they advertise the US/England World Cup match. Luckily I have a tablet, Sling, and a VPN. They did “try” to open a trashed club to show it after I and my new British friends pressed them (as they advertised it” but there was poop all over the restaurant, they would not have a server, couldn’t get the game on the tvs, and the bar itself was not actually cleaned from the night before. Thank god for the internet!

Advertised restaurants were shut for private parties when I showed up. My question about restaurant reservations was answered with “oh no wait, just show up.” The lie detector determined “that was a lie.”

Don’t expect the staff to know a thing about Hyatt benefits either. If you have status, you’ll just get told “we’re completely booked” as a front desk person looks at their phone when the hotel is half empty and you can book any room for that night on Hyatt’s website.

Overall, this place is terrible. Save the headache and learn from my mistake.

5th street prices and the food is pretty bland. It wasn’t terrible, but I probably won’t eat here again. Expect to pay US prices for a meal you could eat at a cantina in a border state.

I had enchiladas and a burrito. The latter was better as the enchiladas were dry. The enchilada sauce was great, but they used very little so they were dry overall.

pin.name: Harry's Steakhouse & Raw Bar | Playa del Carmen

review.text:
If you want to go all out on your holiday and don’t want to talk to your date, this spot is your jam. Unlike their sister restaurants, Harry’s is not my jam: overpriced, too loud with shitty music, and the cotton candy throw in at the end is just…. Weird for a $$$$ type restaurant.

The good: the food is delicious. That’s about all I can say though.

The bad: they brought out a steak cart to show off the cuts. They would have looked delicious 4 hours before, but were all brown and oxidases. At the prices they charge this is unacceptable to me. Small add ons are pricey, think $9 water bottles. The meal ends and they bring cotton candy, the unsupervised all-child table of 10 sitting adjacent gasped when I told the waiter “thanks but no thanks.” Sorry, but pure sugar just doesn’t mesh up after a fancy steak meal and just seems cheap for the price point at this place. Music will be deafening, and it’s very family friendly so if you want to bring your friends and they’re children and set the kiddos at their own table, it’ll be cool here, but again isn’t my jam.

pin.name: Candlewood Suites Washington Dulles Sterling by IHG

review.text: It’s a candlewood so not a posh high end hotel. Pros include price and access to a Walmart right next door. Cons include thin walls and annoyingly loud bathroom fans. If someone with a Hemi parks near your room and gets up every day at 4, expect to be woken up early on a daily basis.

pin.name: Porfirio's Playa del Carmen | Restaurante de comida mexicana

review.text:
I really wish google had more star fidelity for their review system. If I could, I’d give a 4.5 because there are some glaring issues here, but overall compared to the other restaurants on 5th street, it’s easily one of the best.

First I’ll start with the good: a variety of Mexican dishes (not Tex-mex) as well as steaks and seafood. If you want somewhere where you will be waited on hand and foot, this is it. They’ll pull your chair back for you, fold your napkin, full 9 “country club” yards service. In my case I’ll also note I almost never dress up, they still seated me without a second glance which to me is a huge plus (I like good meals, but I’m tired after work and don’t like the silly theatre aspects of some upscale restaurants). The food is also quite good.

Now, the bad: deserts in my experience were stale, especially the tres-leches. Seriously if management reads these, up the game by not using stale cake, the flavor was amazing but tres leches should never feel stale in your mouth. Second, order your steak on the less cooked side than you want, especially if it’s coming on a skillet. They’re batting .333 here from personal experience, but not in a shoe-leather kind of way, more like “I wish it were just a bit bloodier” way.

Overall, I’ll return again and again when I’m back in PDC. Vibe is great, set list on point (albeit generic), and the staff are amazing (seriously, they’re the best part here).

Top notch, seriously you can’t go wrong here (unless you’re on a serious budget). I can’t say Kaskabel is cheap, but the food quality and value rivals any seafood place in the states. Amazing beef cuts, but ordering a whole fish is where it’s at.

The waiters are amazing (shoutout to Berzain)! No English, no problem. Hip tunes in the evenings on the weekend (live DJ) but I’m probably too lame to make these claims myself. My friends however agree so I’m confident in my assessment.

Sabàtic Sitges is a brand new property and may be the best kept secret (for now) in the Marriott portfolio in terms of hotels near Barcelona. Let's start with the good:
1. Amazing breakfast, if you're looking for an American Breakfast you may be disappointed, but this is Spain so live it up with all of the Spanish food you want: Tortilla, jamon serrano, pan y pan dulces (seriously, there is a full table).
2. The hotel is stunning, rooms are large, light and have amazing balconies with very comfortable lounges. The bed/shower were great and there was plenty of room to work and relax.
3. Its right on top of a grocery store. That's a huge plus in my book because I was able to easily grab lunch, snacks, sundries, etc. without ever really "leaving"
4. It's a quick walk to the beach, train station (you can easily take the regional R train to Barcelona).
5. The staff are super helpful and kind
6. It's amazingly quiet, no rowdy Barcelona behaviour to be found here, you can relax in peace.

Now I'll review the bad: really there wasn't anything wrong with my stay. I can't think of a single negative and I'm pretty picky.

Enough said, I'll be staying here again

Overpriced for drinks that aren’t too tasty, but let’s be honest, that’s not dissuading anyone, even me. Expect to spend 20$ for the tall barbel shaped frozen drinks that melt in 20 min if you don’t give yourself terrible brain freeze. 3/5 would probably return to get myself closer to having diabetes.

Decent bill older / worn out. Be prepared for loud ac/fans in the bathroom. Pros -> it’s close to the airport, cons -> not easy to walk to dinner if you’re an Uber in/out kinda guest just there to work. Overall it’s pretty cheap tho so not a bad place to stay for a short trip.

I would avoid Winjet if at all possible if you’re looking for a relaxing or clean trip to the islands. I chose to sit outside, terrible mistake. Each row has a designated number of seats, the family next to me decided to squeeze an additional 2 people over the designated limits… ok I’ll stand. Apparently that’s a safety issue but overcrowding your ferry is not, so I sat for an hour being sat on literally by a sweaty stranger with my arm wrapped around a column because they don’t let you move once the boat is loaded.

If you do choose to forego my warning and choose to sit outside, you’re involuntarily forced to listen to a small concert so you’ll be unable to speak with your travel companions. If you sit inside, be warned the ferries are in rough shape. I’ve taken lots of ferries before, this one is definitely a bottom 3 experience, if not the worst ferry I’ve ever had the “pleasure” of taking.

pin.name: Winjet Fast Ferry Playa del Carmen

review.text:
I would avoid Winjet if at all possible if you’re looking for a relaxing or clean trip to the islands. I chose to sit outside, terrible mistake. Each row has a designated number of seats, the family next to me decided to squeeze an additional 2 people over the designated limits… ok I’ll stand. Apparently that’s a safety issue but overcrowding your ferry is not, so I sat for an hour being sat on literally by a sweaty stranger with my arm wrapped around a column because they don’t let you move once the boat is loaded.

If you do choose to forego my warning and choose to sit outside, you’re involuntarily forced to listen to a small concert so you’ll be unable to speak with your travel companions. If you sit inside, be warned the ferries are in rough shape. I’ve taken lots of ferries before, this one is definitely a bottom 3 experience, if not the worst ferry I’ve ever had the “pleasure” of taking.

The Thompson Main House is in the right spot in the right place, but has a far way to go for a full 5 stars. I waffled a bit between 3/4 but ultimately it’s a very nice property, and some of my complaints with the hard product are simply something the hotel can’t do anything about.

I’ll start with the bad so if you’re skimming while deciding where to stay you don’t have to read several paragraphs. Bathrooms are glass so if you wake up in the middle of the night you’re either going in the dark or waking up other occupants. Rooms do not have a desk, don’t kid yourself it’ll be easy to work here even with the “work from Hyatt” package.

If you envisioned watching the World Cup here in your room, think again. They advertised that they would have it on upstairs but don’t have the tv package to have it on the bar meaning I had to scramble at 5am. This is a huge oversight, when pressed I was told “that package costs money” never mind this is not a cheap property nor the fact most bars in Latam have it on and are definitely not as fancy. If your room package includes a cabana/bed/etc at the rooftop club, expect to repeat yourself multiple times for a reservation. Mine mysteriously was never made never mind speaking with the front desk 3x and concierge 2x, oh yea and when I went day off and made the reservation upon return 5 minutes after confirmation there were people in my space. Now I’m the bad guy for asking to have them removed when staff should’ve been able to mark it off. I know this isn’t a $300+ a night property, but it’s playa which has a lower average cost so I’m disappointed.

The good: the hotel is beautiful. Rooms are nicely laid out and tasteful. This is a hotel in a vacation spot so If that’s why you’re here you’ll have what you need and are in an amazing location.

Be warned, it stays loud till 3am. Light sleepers should choose to stay elsewhere.

This is the place. Quick service, nice guys, great prices. If you’re from the states, save the haircut appointment for your vacay and have 40$ left over to blow on sweet sweet Mexican food and cold beers.

There are better restaurants around which are far cheaper. Food isn’t half bad, service is ok. Expect to pay the tourist tax here.

Hound them for your change, they won’t bring it and you’ll be ripped off. TVs are broken but at least they had the World Cup on.

Great restaurant with an amazing sunset view, good food, and very reasonable prices. I’d give 5 stars but the end of my meal was interrupted by a fire dancer routine which was really cool to watch at first but the fumes were absolutely unbearable. I think they were using gasoline, to be honest I’m not sure but actually had to leave as it was so bad as someone who suffers from asthma.
Want to have a beer on top of a centuries old city wall? This is your place. No covering so best enjoyed as the sun is lower in the sky.

I’m honestly unsure how this place is rated 4.9 stars, I can’t understand how anyone would find fault with anything. Before I even start with how good the food is, I’ll note the ambiance and decor are on point. Open kitchen concept means you can watch the chefs work their magic.

The food is amazing, beyond the ceviches which were amazing, the appetizers were great. I tried the ceviche tipico, pulpo asado, and tequeños de lomo. The ceviche had great texture and was super fresh. The pulpo cooked just right, ie not rubbery or tough. The tequeños were a wild card, but the sweet sauce paired w a crunchy texture and salty filling absolute perfection.

If you miss this place in Cartagena you are missing out on life.

Craft beer garden with their own beers/ciders on tap. Low key vibe that has great service and amazing tunes playing. Food is typical bar-bite-esque, but you can have a full meal here. House beers were pretty good as well.

Amazing selection of meats, particularly dry aged steaks. Not cheap for Colombia, but a bargain compared to the US or EU and the quality is very very high.

4th floor setting is nice, my only (very minor) complaint is the music is quite loud so it may be hard to talk with others at your table.

Yatai Market is a great setting for a group of friends getting together. Each person can get exactly what they want. Fare options include mostly asian street style dishes such as bao, rice bowls, ramen, etc.
Lower prices, fewer people, and better food than nearby Mercado San Miguel. Only downside is the hours are not as long here, by 5 most kiosks are closed.
Great cut, super affordable. It’s a non reservation system, show up, take a number, get cut.

Would be a 4 star, but dirty bottle necks knock it down a star. Sliders were pretty decent but a bit overpriced (then again, it’s Dubrovnik). Tacos were stale and served cold. The Bottle rust is pretty nasty tho to be honest.

Stopped by based on the smells and decided to have lunch before walking around the old town. The good, no waiting, no reservation, no issues. The bad, the salmon tartar was not very fresh, the truffle tenderloin had little to no truffle, and the polenta was bland.

Overall it’s dubrovnik, it’s not a meal you’ll treasure as a memory, but you could do worse.

Surprising that it’s 3*. The aquarium is about 9$ which for Dubrovnik is a serious steal, all of the fish look well kept, and it’s within the city walls which is super cool. It’s not an attraction you’ll spend all day at, but it’s a cool place to spend half an hour.
This place has the games! If you want to watch football (both kinds), catch a bit of baseball, or other sporting events that aren’t Hajduk or the Croatian National team this is the spot.

Probably the best sushi place in Split. The restaurant has a very nice terrace, and unlike the restaurants in the old town is not stupidly crowded. The prices are on par with what you will pay for sushi in Croatia.

pin.name: Baby Jane Cocktail House & Noodle Bar

review.text: Great spot on the promenade in Trogir. Prices are fair (25-30$ for a steak plate). Service is great, and the food is delivered quickly.
Great food, cooked fast, and not as crowded as some of the other restaurants nearby.
It’s Whole Foods, so expect to spend your whole paycheck on a grocery run. In return you’ll eat the latest and greatest healthy trendy foods. It’s a pretty good experience overall.
Expensive, but that's Montevideo for you. It's a nice place to hang out, prices are ok at best.
Fun place to go with great seating. Many food options are available so everyone can pick and choose what they'd like to eat.
Run down property, smells a bit like weed inside.

Don't bother trying to buy beer here or for that matter, anything requiring an id unless you have a Texas drivers license. Cashiers are uneducated on ID types (federal, other states, and foreign). Was told I was using a fake id, they refused to scan it. Oh well, guess I saved myself a few dollars even if I wasted half an hour trying to find another convenience store near my hotel. Welcome to good ole uneducated Texas and another reminder of why I left.

The food is tasty, but the Happy Hour spicy tuna roll came out a little misshapen. At the end of the day it’s all going to look the same post digestion, but if you care about the presentation, it’s not the fanciest looking,

My rec: Go for happy hour and snag a bucket of beers for $20 which is a bargain in this area! I’d avoid sitting outside if you need WiFi, the connection speed is slow. Try some of the fusion rolls: I’d never personally choose to include plantains in my sushi, but I asked for a suggestion and it was delicious. The wait staff are all super nice and also bilingual (English y Español) which is a huge plus!

Salads are where it’s at. If you want a bowel of green laden w sashimi I highly recommend. My main complaint is that the beers are stupidly expensive compared to the rest of the menu, but then again it’s Brickell in Miami 🤷.

Happy hour menu is limited, but decent enough. The WiFi is blazing fast in case you’re like me and a visitor without a us SIM card. Service is great and waitstaff are all bilingual.

pin.name: Mac Elaviv Coolsingel DönerKebab / Shoarma & Nightshop-Avondwinkel

review.text: Tasty, it can’t be healthy based on how delicious my kebab was.

Decent hotel at a good price. The neighborhood around is a bit iffy so I would not wander around at night. It's a bit far from the airport so plan to take an uber unless the shuttle is super conveniently timed for you.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express Rotterdam - Central Station by IHG

review.text: Decent hotel centrally located. It's a bit tired (carpet is worn and could use replacement) but for the money is a good value.
Not much available in store and staff always seem to be busy. Best to order online and have delivered.
Easily the best of the food hall style "Mercados" in Montevideo. The Taco stall and pizzas are especially on point. Good for a large group as you can get a massive table and sit together.
It's a Hard Rock Cafe, the music, pins, and ambiance are why you visit. Food was a bit overcooked/ dry when I visited. The music playlist was on point.
Amazing set of shops and stalls with "street food." Warning, it closes a bit early so is best visited during the day.
Fun area to hang out. Around lunchtime there are lots of popups with a variety of things to eat.
Fun foodhall with apartments making up the building. It’s a bit more expensive than other places but there is lots of variety so everyone should find something good to eat.

Top notch meal. The food is served quickly, steaks are a reasonable price, meal was delicious.

There is a lovely place to sit outside and the restaurant seems to never be super packed. We made a reservation for Sunday night but it wasn’t really needed.

Decent hotel but extremely disorganized. I stayed here with several colleagues for a week of working collaboratively together. It was difficult at best to get pricing from staff for a conference room.

Unfortunately the conference room was not prepared, we needed to arrange and ask for the screen and such to be setup.

Overall, average at best.

Don’t expect much. The Moxy in Stratford may be a Marriott property, but it’s definitely not a great one.

I usually choose Moxy properties due to the working area. This one has one, but it tends to be used by random people off the street doing whatever. The bathrooms (mens at least) smell accordingly to the point it’s overwhelming. They also love to crank the music in the lobby to the point you have to shout. Not a good place for a business stay overall.

Rooms are decent at best. The aircon will work ok if it’s below 24/26. Don’t expect a cold room during heat waves. Carpet is stained all over and not in great condition.

There are signs all over about drugs, but I witnessed several deals in front of and around the property. Expect the lobby or even your rooms to reek of weed at least once if you stay longer than a day. They do have “security” who are more intent on asking the obvious business travelers working past 10 if they are staying there rather than concentrating on the people who are obviously not staying at the hotel.

Overall terrible hotel, I don’t think I’ll return.

Amazing property, well located in Cologne, especially for the price. Rooms were large and spacious. The hotel is super convenient to Köln Messe and 10 min across the river from the main station. Other than a cancelled train, getting here was a breeze.

If you’re spending a weekend, including breakfast in your rate is a wise move, there is not much around open early on weekend mornings plus the buffet spread is well better than average for Germany (full English, pancakes, waffles, 4 egg choices, bread/fruit/cold cuts spread). Most of the restaurant action is on the other side of the river, but there are a couple good options within 5-10 min walking as well.

My suggestion, spend the money and splurge on the club floor, the view of the cathedral and river during breakfast are worth the upgrade price.

It’s a Hard Rock Cafe, they’re all rather the same, but this was one of the better ones. Prices seemed a bit lower (10€ for a Messi burger) than typical for a Hard Rock. The staff were all friendly and the rotation of music was fun.
Striking exhibits. I visited with several friends who didn’t know what to expect from a “War Museum” upon leaving one remarked “maybe it’s better called an impact of war museum.”

It’s a glorified advertisement for Lindt, but they give you free chocolate. All kidding aside it’s a cool place to learn a bit about your favorite indulgence. Exhibits include the history of chocolate, sustainability practices, a working production line, and a giant chocolate fountain. Definitely a great place for kids!

Beautiful new property. voco The Hague sets a high bar for new IHG hotels. Breakfast (included in my rate due to status) was smaller in offering, but high quality and very complete. The rooms were large, bright, airy, and well laid out.

Some small nitpicks that don’t detract enough to give less than 5 stars: there is nowhere in the bathroom to hang a towel. This meant I threw mine on the floor causing it to be rewatched when I’d normally hang it to dry. The sliding bathroom doors slide open fogging up the room as you shower.

Everything else was marvelous. Ps for business travelers there’s a very well laid out space downstairs to work.
Food is decent, low key local vibe (not many tourists).
Very German in all aspects. Come here for an authentic no nonsense experience.
Really nice selection of beers on tap, live music on Sunday afternoon, and great food.

Amazing steaks at a great price (20£ for a 300g ribeye 👀. All in I spent about 40£ for 2 steaks & sides. The crew working there was great, and the steaks were cooked perfectly. I will definitely return next time I’m in the area.
Location is great and they have outside seating, but some of the staff are a bit off and prices are high. There are plenty of other nearby pubs with a better vibes.

Amazing customer service. My suitcase was on its last legs. Try as I might to get another trip out if it, it was time. I went in early on a Sunday morning to see what they had. While they did not have what I was originally looking for in stock, Olivia spent the time to pull all of their suitcases out, explain pros and cons of each brand then followed up with me later that day regarding what they had readily available. Delivery was quick and straight to the hotel I was staying at so I didn't have to lug my purchase around town on a Sunday funday.

Average experience, but the vibe and layout is nice. Limited selection of stalls. Food is priced appropriately for London. I wouldn’t go out of my way to eat here again, but when I’m nearby it’s a decent enough spot.

pin.name: DF Tacos x Market Halls Canary Wharf

review.text: I was honestly surprised to see the low ratings. My tacos were quick, reasonably priced, and delicious. Only complaint is tacos should be on corn tortillas and the flour ones used were not the freshest. Then again, I lived in Mexico for awhile so am picky about my tacos. 🤷‍♂️
Food is pretty decent, prices are reasonable for London even if it’s in the heart of the touristy area.
Delightful pub that’s a far cry from the hustle and bustle of Canary Wharf.
Quick, cheap, and easy. Food is pretty bland and looks a little unnatural in color. Taste matches
Quick, convenient, and connected. Jump right off the tube to your office and grab lunch on the way.
Bummer there are no tables here any more. Takeaway only, the coffee is pretty good though.
It’s Itsu, you know what you’re going to get… decent option for a quick lunch, cheap, relatively healthy. Line can get long sometimes at this location.

Is it in the heart of a touristy area? Yes. Can it get crowded and body to body? Yes. Is it more expensive than a bap at Pret? Yes. Still, Brood is a great place to sit and watch the tourists go by. Food is on par for the price paid, staff are always patient with the tourists, and the food comes quickly.

Hip and trendy place that seems more style than substance. Restaurant is a bit dirty and my drink tasted off. Restroom had dryers let out a foul smell, so don’t bother using them. Signs saying to watch your drink make me worry about the type of crowd this place attracts when I come with female friends…

Always fresh, delicious, and served quickly. This is a non-traditional sushi restaurant so don’t expect an authentic omakase, however the quality is amazing. Be prepared that you will pay for the quality. A light lunch will set you back £30/person easily. Splurging of course costs more.

Hard 70s department store vibe at this hard rock. I went for brunch with friends prior to seeing The Rolling Stones at Hyde Park. What better way to start the vibe right? I think they missed the mark with the decor, it’s nice but so big you don’t have any memorabilia sitting next to you. The entertainers were on point tho.

Food is typical Hard Rock quality, if you’ve eaten it at one you have eaten the same thing at all of them. The service at this location is on par, it’s just really busy. Expect a wait to be seated.
It’s a budget hotel and it shows. Our room was not terribly clean and our sink and shower were clogged our entire stay. No free breakfast for Marriott status members either.

I stayed at the Marriott City Centre in Bristol for 3 weeks so take that into account with my review (I had a lot more time to notice the little things). If I’m in Bristol again I would definitely return.

The good: the staff are lovely, the exec lounge is usually well appointed, rooms are large for the uk, and the nightly rate is fair. Beds are comfortable, toiletries provided were great, and the park views lovely. Honestly I’m a bit sad to leave because even with the minor issues I’ll outline the staff are super friendly and always worked to help me out when needed.

The bad: the carpet is dated and threadbare in places. The kitchen and bar close a bit early for my taste. Check the dishes at breakfast, every few days I’d notice a dirty plate or glass.

The ugly: the hotel has a cool terrace, but it’s underutilized. AC in the exec lounge is non existent if it’s super hot.
Typical Wagamama experience. I love the food, but it’s often slow and this location is no exception.
The smells don’t lie. Quick, affordable, & delicious!

The guys working here are amazing. I dropped my older iPad and didn’t feel like purchasing another. 60quid later and my tablet is fresher looking with a new screen. Will return if I ever break a mobile device while on holiday.
Stopped in for a snack, chicken satay was small and pretty dry but otherwise had good flavor. Fried spring rolls were better, but not amazing.
Decent spot for us style barbecue. The sauces were a bit sweet for my taste, but I can’t knock a star off as everything else was very good and sauce preferences can be a regional thing.
Decent variety of brews on tap and prices are reasonable 4-6£/ pour.
This location seems to be one of the better wagamamas. The staff is attentive & service was quick. Food is par for the course and no different than any other location.
Decent meals, relatively quick, upscale ambiance.
Great place right by St Nicholas Market. Food was fresh, quick, and fairly priced. Portions are not huge, but that’s ok.

pin.name: Bansky's Girl with a Pierced Eardrum

review.text: Very cool to see in person, it’s locked behind a gate so you can’t get close but that’s a good thing. Easy to get to if you are hanging out around the wharfs on the weekend.
Decent prices, great spot, and the food is fairly good.
Quick, cheap, healthy. What more can you want?
Decent place to sit and watch the foot traffic go by. Card only, no cash.
Nice little place for a beer. I have not tried the food, but if the weather is good it's a great place to grab a drink, stand around & chat with your friends, and watch the traffic go by.
Food is merely ok, but prices are low. You go here for the breathtaking view of the sea.

pin.name: Hyatt Centric Midtown 5th Avenue New York

review.text: Room was nicely sized for NYC and the hotel well appointed for the price. I have to knock off 1 star for the horribly loud AC and trash left on the balcony.

pin.name: Holiday Inn Express Newark Airport – Elizabeth by IHG

review.text: You’re probably staying here because it’s near the Newark airport. Out of all the proximate hotels, I probably won’t stay here again. Internet is very very slow, rooms are a bit dated, and the airport shuttle only takes you as far as the air train which added 20-30 minutes onto getting into the terminal at 4:30 am. Not the worst stay ever, but very far from the best.
Quality is great, prices are low, speed terrific. What more could you want from takeaway?
Food is pretty decent, expect to pay a bit more than average but well worth it,

Ebisu/The Square are seriously top notch. It’s hard to find really good sushi in the Balkans. Ebisu ups the game of the entire area significantly. Prices are high compared to local restaurants but the quality rivals any global city.

Expect to spend upwards of $30/person, but the food is amazing. Ambiance is great, and the staff extremely professional.

Bartenders are super friendly and they have a nice selection mirroring an Irish pub. Specifically I mean they’ve got something for everyone: it’s not a craft beer bar with only smaller label taps. If you want an Urquell or Hoegarden you’ll be pleased as will your friends who want something like a local IPA or stout. Mixed drinks are also on point.

Comunale is amazing, hands down. Bread before a meal may be standard at some places, the bread basket Comunale provides takes it to another level, I’d suggest going for the pasta or pizza dishes over the meats. The meat dishes are great, and I’m splitting hairs when I say I’ve had better elsewhere.

Prices are incredibly reasonable for a Michelin Star restaurant, expect to spend $40-$60 for 2 with drinks, and an app or some sides. If you head to Belgrade, you can’t afford to not visit.
Lunch was amazing. End of review.
Great steaks! The atmosphere is upscale and the clientele skew older. If you want to go on a Friday or Saturday make a reservation as it gets crowded.
Nice spot to stop in, have a beer, and enjoy the fresh air. The music is a bit loud, but prices are reasonable (4$/beer) and staff are friendly and quick.

I got a weird look ordering just a steak and a beer for myself. Hands down, this was one of the best steaks I’ve had in Spain and it was only 22 euros. I almost ordered a second one for desert, I’ll blame my dinner companion for that.

My vegetarian friend ordered some type of less meat inclined meal that consisted of some type of pasta with red sauce and cheese.I figured I should have at least a bite and ate half her plate, this no desert steak. I’ll definitely return here, amazing meal.
We really loved the team that was working when we stopped in! Food was great, service greater!

Quaint coffee shop that could be out of a fantasy novel. The space is small but service quick. Cakes are delicious and the shop has a wide variety of fancy foods to go. Prices are a bit on the high side, but not completely unreasonable.
Very good food.

pin.name: Four Points by Sheraton Barcelona Diagonal

review.text:
I've stayed in this Four Points countless times visiting Barcelona. It's usually by far the most economical of the nicer hotel chains in Barcelona and almost always about half the price of other comparable hotels nearby.

Some pros to this location include the fact that its in Poblenou, so there is excellent dining around, and the area isn't jam packed with tourists like you will find around the Gothic Quarter or other popular neighborhoods. Rooms on the upper floors tend to have a pretty good view, and are a nice size.

On the "Cons" side, the current "COVID" reality we live in has meant a few changes (such as no bar), but overall the staff have managed to ensure that the offering is as good as it was pre-covid. Breakfast is on the more basic side, but if you have Platinum or higher status with Marriott it's free.

Quiet place that does not seem to get alot of traffic. Tacos were competent, cooked quickly and great. I give them extra points for using corn tortillas on the Tacos al Pastor I ordered (it seems like flour tortillas are the norm in Europe), but they aren't from un trompo so don't get your hopes up for something you would find in CDMX. Then again, this isn't Mexico.
The monkeys here don’t disappoint. Aggressive and with venereal disease they love to meet new hikers.

Food is nothing to write home about, and is just as expensive as any other local restaurant. Still, it has that vibe you expect from a Hard Rock, and the live bands aren’t bad to enjoy a beer or two over.
Food is quick, cheap, and delicious. Wraps were my favorite, particularly the falafel.
Karlssons has some of the best European food in Patong hands down. That said, our meals were definitely on the higher end for Thailand.
Google should mark as closed. There are plenty of other “night markets” in Phuket with lots of stalls.

If you’re visiting Phuket and like the grandiose you should definitely stop by. Be advised you must dress appropriately so covered knees/shoulders are a must (coverings count). With the taxi situation in Phuket it’s best to hire a driver if you didn’t rent your own car, and see this with several other local sites in a 1 day excursion.

I’ll start with the pros: the staff were all amazing, the location excellent, and the property in top shape. In a way I wish could give 4.9 as the Hilton Diagonal Mar is not quite the full 5 stars on the fact that the breakfast is limited compared to the competition at this price point, and guest rooms feeling a bit dated. Overall it’s a nice place to stay and it’s definitely on my lists of properties to return to. It’s just a bit boring, but that’s ok.

pin.name: Four Points by Sheraton Phuket Patong Beach Resort

review.text:
The Four Points in Patong is a great hotel, but it’s just not quite up to five stars yet. If I had to give one glaring fault to this location, I’d say it’s understaffed. That translates to a lot of waiting around for services that should be basic and quick at a hotel if this caliber. Some examples include:

1: need something from the front desk like your key to be redone when it quits working? Expect to spend 20-30 minutes in line.
2: want coffee with your breakfast? If it shows up, more than half the time it takes close to 30 minutes.
3: need to call the front desk for anything? If they answer you’ll need to have tried 5-6 times minimum.

Now some of the good: the property is beautiful and well maintained. Rooms are spacious, modern, and well laid out. The on site breakfast buffet is expansive with lots of options for everyone. There are enough elevators that it’s not a huge problem to get a solo one during the pandemic as well.

Now some of the negative points beyond the under staffing issue: walls to the main hallways are thin and there are definitely some loud guests late into the evening. Our tv cable channels did not work, if you have Marriott status you probably won’t get an upgrade and suite night redemptions weren’t possible either. The hotel is a bit far from the center of patong so expect to walk or grab a car.

Overall the hotel is pleasant and on the cusp of being a great property, it just seems there are still a few teething issues to work through.

Aura and her laundry service are amazing! Aura was super warm, always friendly, amazingly quick pickup service and the finished product was clean, neatly pressed, and tidily wrapped. I highly recommend her if you are staying in patong!
Cool hidden spot. It’s a typical beach dive bar or dive beach bar? Be aware this is a cash only establishment. First come first served seating so get there early for the best sunset views.

Staff were very friendly and offered great advice on getting a good massage nearby. The food was cooked well and delivered quickly. Prices were not cheap, but completely reasonable. Bathrooms were clean. The view of the sea is a bit blocked, but that’s ok, they offer a spacious patio.

Beach is ok, if you are staying in Patong it’s probably not worth the trip. It’s the same bay of water, just as crowded, 100thb cover, and of course you have to get there which means driving or a tuk tuk (unless you’re stupid like me and walk there ignoring googles suggestion that there are a lot of hills on the way). FYI -> The walk there is ok, just not for the lazy (4km, steep up/down grade basically the whole way).

If you are staying at the Marriott Merlin Beach I might make the trek. Then again, the shuttle into Patong for the public beach is free, the beach just as good, and there are more food options.

Last comm went -> the restrooms (men’s at least) had no soap (I didn’t personally inspect the women’s so no clue). Also no TP, but there is a hose bidet so all good. The soap part is pretty nasty in a global pandemic and a restroom in general, thus my reason for 3 vs 4 stars.

Food perfectly cooked, staff were amazing, and incredible view! There are a handful of similar restaurants along the same strip between Paradise Beach and where the hotels start. I’ve tried several of them and Paradise offered the most value to me as the prices are a tad lower and food quality similar.
Great service & reasonable prices. In Thailand it’s nice to occasionally take a break from local food and just have a salad. Wine Connection is a great choice with a variety of bowls/salads/etc.

I’m not a huge wrap fan, but the wraps at Beer Sound really hit the spot for a light 2nd lunch. Decor/vibe are great (think 1950s us garage/rockabilly), but a bit strange for a turkish/Thai restaurant. Nonetheless, nice staff, quick service & reasonable prices.

Property is definitely aging (alarm clock has an old iPod connection) and expensive for Mexico City. The location is good, right across from Chapultepec Park and around a ton of great places to eat. I’m a Spire elite member, so for those looking for upgrades/perks don’t expect much. I was “upgraded to a park view room” which was really no different or better than having the Polanco side as your view.
Food selection is limited and the lounge is smaller and dated but the staff are nice. At 275 pesos to enter (usd $14) it’s better than hanging in the terminal and relatively quiet.

Conveniently located and you can find what you need. Two major pros for this location:
1 - it’s in a historic building and you can see the original Corinthian columns in the middle of the produce which has led to UNESCO protection.
2 - very decent hot/ Pre prepared food counter where you can get an inexpensive meal.

The downside, a couple of the cashiers hate tourists and are very very rude without reason. I stayed in Split and it was not a one off occurrence.
If you visit the Trevi fountain, this is the place to stop for a snack. Inexpensive & delicious. Not much more to say.
It's an Obelisk, in public. Cool thing to see thus the 5 stars, but I wouldn't plan my vacation around it.

Worst 24 hours ever. I could end my review at that, but in the interest of sharing with other travelers I will expand on my statement. Unless you are taking a car long distance or have no other option I would avoid Grimaldi lines.

I took the ferry from Barcelona to Civitavecchia in July 2021. During a global pandemic I will start by saying there is absolutely no way to socially distance unless you confine yourself to one of the very dirty cabins that is not only cramped, but completely disgusting. I've taken alot of ferries in my travels, and this one was by far the most tired, worn out, dirty ship I've ever been on.

Bring several books or something to do, there was absolutely no wifi, and once you get away from the coast there is obviously no mobile reception. I never look to a ferry for a Michelin star dining experience, but after the boredom the terrible food is the next worse thing. I sprung for the "premier" dining option my first night and paid 30 euros for a dinner that was cold and obviously came out of a vacuum sealed bag. There wasn't even an attempt to make it appetizing. I figured I would stick to sandwiches, but those too were also unappetizing, dry, and generally tasteless.

That leaves not much else to do but maybe drink for the voyage and hope it passes quickly. The ferry I took was hosting a Zumba cruise of some kind the day I was on it. During a pandemic there were hundreds of Zumba-ers in close quarters blaring terrible music all day long. Super spreader event anyone?

I love taking ferries, but will never again take Grimaldi in my life.
Zlatna Ribica is always a great place to grab a cheap seafood meal. Food is fresh, cooked quickly, and staff are friendly! Be aware, it is a cash only establishment
Mediocre sushi at best. The atmosphere is nice, and the view great. Come for that, the food could definitely be better and was nothing to write home about.

This might be the best restaurant in Split. For a Michelin star restaurant the prices were more than reasonable ($50 for a bottle of wine, steak, and cuttlefish risotto). Make sure you get a reservation the night before, space is usually limited.

It’s hard to find a meal that doesn’t break the bank in Dubrovnik. If you are traveling on a budget, this is a great place to stop. Portions are big enough to share and fried up quickly! Only downside is the restaurant is a tad small so there may be a wait at times.
Food here is always great. I especially recommend the truffle pasta. Sit outside overlooking the harbor for a particularly nice sunset view in the evening if the weather is nice!

Amazing risotto, very professional staff. One of the major pluses for this restaurant is the blankets they have at all the tables outside as it does get chilly in the evenings here. The gold on the shrimp was a bit much, but the flavors were on point.

pin.name: Crowne Plaza Rome - St. Peter's by IHG

review.text: Hotel is well kept and the staff lovely. One downside is the location is a bit far away from the main tourist attractions in Rome. Next time I come for a weekend away, I will probably stay a little closer in, but overall the hotel was quite nice.
Why eat anything other than Italian food when I’m Rome? This place is definitely a good reason to! Arepas were great, meat had lots of flavor, definitely needed extra napkins!
Wow, cheapest meal I’ve had in a European capital in years. At 5/6 euros per dish I will definitely stop in again next time I visit the Vatican!!!
Amazing pasta, very inexpensive!
Amazing pasta, great prices, quaint vibe! Deep fried pizza was especially good and very cheap at 3 euros!
Beautiful views & great drinks!
Food was great, large beers were cold & delicious, and the staff were super kind and polite!
Quick, cheap, and pretty good!

I would highly advise against ever staying at this place. The room was filthy, the area outside the hotel is littered, and the staff don’t seem to care at all. I understand this is one of Marriott’s lower tier brands, but figured the affiliation would count for something.

I waited for the better part of an hour for front desk to show up while in a rush jotting between meetings. Stay somewhere else, even if the location is convenient.
5 people behind the counter, 1-2 actually working. Terrible customer service.
Great service, convenient location & high quality product. These guys have been awesome getting small print jobs turned around for us quickly!

Great guys working here, and a nice selection of brands like Seven, Gunnar, Cervelo, Pinarello, and Santa Cruz for the bike nerd like myself. I've always received great service here and they also sponsor some really cool rides/ clubs/ events.
